title: Contract Specialist (GS-11)
description_text: This position serves as a Contract Specialist within the Office of the Chief Operating Officer, Library of Congress, Washington, D. C. The incumbent plans, solicits, awards and administers contracts for a number of Service Units within the Library.

description_text: This position located in the Facility Operations Office, Facility Services, Integrated Support Services (ISS), Library of Congress (LC). The incumbent supports the management of facilities and equipment within the Library of Congress, focusing on Library facilities to include physical structures, utilities, surrounding grounds, custodial and food services. This position performs a variety of administrative services to inspect, maintain, repair/modify facilities, and to operate facility…

The incumbent conceives, plans, researches, writes, complies, edits, and proofreads publications and informational materials for blind and physically handicapped users of the Library of Congress free reading program, a national network of cooperating libraries, consumer groups, and the public.Consideration under…

The Secretary is responsible for providing exemplary customer service in the form of greeting and registering employees…

The incumbent coordinates the work of digital conversion specialists assigned to divisions in LCSG, reporting to the Chief of…

description_text: Availability: Applicants must be a current Congressional Research Service (CRS) employee serving in a permanent or indefinite position at the GS-11 or above. Duration: Depending on the qualifications of the applicant selected, this may be a temporary promotion, NTE 120 days or a detail with the possibility of an extension. More than one applicant may be selected to serve on a rotating basis. Tour…

The position reports directly to the Chief of the Collection Division and performs project management duties related to the planning, development, and delivery of Collection Division strategic goals, initiatives, and special projects.

The incumbent assists in preparing and maintaining the official records of the section related to ordering, shipping, receiving, storage, and inventory control. Supports maintenance of the complete inventory…

The incumbent serves under the administrative supervision of the Director of the Global Legal Research Directorate. The incumbent works highly independently, exercising considerable judgment and carries out duties and…

Recommends policies and procedures and assists…

The person selected for this position will be placed on the position description that corresponds with their qualifications. The position serves as a member of the Channel Design and Deployment (CDD) section of the Program Delivery Division (PDD) of the National Library Service for Blind and Print Disabled (NLS) and works under the…

title: Work Study Student Program 2025/2026 (GS-01)
description_text: The Library of Congress Work Study Student Program is a progressive, career development program for local high school students. It combines on-the-job training and career development with classroom instructions and education. Under the program, students earn a federal salary, receive work site supervision and career assistance. Students will get the chance to work part-time when school is in session with the possibility of full-time…

It is the policy of the Library to recruit and hire qualified applicants with targeted disabilities by assisting these individuals in obtaining employment consistent with their level of skills and abilities and their capacity for safe and efficient job…

title: Procurement Technician (GS-05)
description_text: The position is located in the Office of Contracts and Grants Management (OCGM) within the Office of the Chief Operating Officer (COO) at the Library of Congress. The incumbent directly reports to the Supervisory Contract Officer and works under the guidance of the Office Automation Assistant in handling a variety of issues, gathering sufficient information from contractors and clients to identify problems, determine the…

title: Communications Director
description_text: The Office of Congressman Pat Ryan (NY-18) seeks an experienced and creative Communications Director to lead a dynamic communications operation in a fast-paced, proactive, and collaborative office. The Communications Director will be responsible for developing and executing a comprehensive communications strategy that advances the Representative’s legislative priorities and strengthens connections with local and national media, with a significant emphasis on digital as well. The position will require working non-traditional hours. Ties to New York’s 18th District are a plus. Responsibilities include: Drafting and editing press releases, op-eds, newsletters, talking points, speeches, and other written materials. Managing the Representative’s digital presence, including social media platforms, website, and multimedia content. Producing and overseeing video, graphics, and other creative content to expand the office’s reach. Pitching the Representative for media opportunities, op-eds, interviews, and speaking engagements. Coordinating interviews, press conferences, and public events in Washington, D.C. and in New York’s 18th Congressional District. Overseeing and managing a robust franking operation, including newsletters, e-blasts, and other constituent communications in compliance with House rules. Monitoring press coverage, providing media analysis, and advising the Representative and staff on messaging. Collaborating with legislative and district staff to ensure consistent, effective communication of policy initiatives and constituent services. Supervising press staff, interns, and district communications support. The pay range for this position is $95,000 – $105,000 annually, commensurate with experience. This position is based in Washington, D.C., with occasional travel to the district as needed, and reports to the Chief of Staff. The office is an equal opportunity employer; we do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, gender identity or expression, or sexual orientation. Candidates of diverse backgrounds are strongly encouraged to apply. To apply, please send your resume, cover letter, and 3 references in a single PDF to ny18resumes@mail.house.gov with the job title and your full name in the subject line (e.g., “Communications Director – First Last”). Applicants will be evaluated on a rolling basis.

title: NM-02 Summer Communications Fellow
description_text: The Office of Congressman Gabe Vasquez seeks a part-time, temporary Communications Fellow for the summer (May through August, exact dates flexible). The Communications Fellow is an integral member of the Congressman’s office and the communications team. Your role is essential to the office’s overall mission of communicating the Congressman and his staff’s work on behalf of New Mexicans to his constituents and the press. The Communications Fellow will primarily be responsible for: Drafting written materials including press releases, e-newsletters, social media content Designing graphics Creating short video clips as needed Building press lists Competitive candidates will possess excellent writing and graphic design skills, creativity, attention to detail and relevant prior experience. This is a part-time, temporary position, with 20-25 hours of work per week expected. The schedule is flexible, and a hybrid remote and in-person work arrangement is preferred. Fully remote applicants will also be considered. Fellows will be paid a monthly stipend. To be considered, please send a resume, cover letter, writing sample, and two digital samples with the subject line “NM-02 Spring Communications Fellow” to NM02Press@mail.house.gov by March 25, 2026. No calls or drop-ins, please. The office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, national origin, disability, military status, age, marital status, or parental status.

title: Summer Washington, D.C. Intern
description_text: Representative Vasquez (NM-02) is seeking qualified Summer 2026 Congressional Interns to work out of the DC office. This internship is designed for college students, recent graduates and young professionals. Applicants must be able to work in a fast-paced environment, have dedication to public service, ability to multitask and attention to detail. New Mexico ties are preferred, but not required. Intern tasks may include, but are not limited to: answering phones, greeting office visitors, scheduling and leading Capitol tours, logging constituent correspondence, drafting letters, working on research projects, and attending congressional briefings and hearings. Additionally, interns may have the opportunity to specialize in either legislative or communications related assignments, based on their interest. This office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate based on race, religion, disability, age, ethnicity, national origin, sex, gender, LGBTQ identification, family status, or military status. Women, people of color, and other members of traditionally underrepresented communities are strongly encouraged to apply. Summer internships in Washington, DC are open on a rolling basis until April 3rd, 2026. Interns in the DC office are required to work a minimum of 30 hours a week. Interns will be paid a monthly stipend based on part-time or full-time status.

title: Summer 2026 District Office Internship
description_text: The Office of Congressman Sean Casten (IL-06) is accepting Summer 2026 internship applications for our Lombard, Illinois office. This paid internship is open to qualified undergraduate or graduate students, or recent college graduates. The summer district office interns will assist with the day-to-day workings and operations of the office, including, but not limited to: Answering constituent phone calls Attending virtual webinars held by different government agencies Assisting staff on various district-based projects and events Drafting and responding to constituent correspondence Interns will learn about constituent relations and will assist with frontline operations, network with staff, and get hands-on experience with casework and outreach. Interns will also conduct research for our outreach and casework teams, including through list building, community news tracking, local issue background research, and event brainstorming. The Lombard Office operates Monday through Friday 9:00 am to 5:00 pm Central Time. The internship program will run from June 1, 2026 to August 31, 2026. Those interested can apply HERE , where they will be required to submit a resume and cover letter. Individuals with district ties are strongly encouraged to apply. Applications will be accepted on a rolling basis until positions are filled. The priority deadline to apply is April 1, 2026, but applications will continue to be considered until May 1, 2026 at 5:00 pm CT.

title: Summer 2026 DC Office Legislative Internship
description_text: The Office of Congressman Sean Casten (IL-06) is accepting Summer 2026 internship applications for our Washington, D.C. office. This paid internship is open to qualified undergraduate or graduate students, or recent college graduates. Legislative interns will assist with the day-to-day workings and operations of the office, including, but not limited to: Answering constituent phone calls Attending committee hearings and briefings Working with legislative and communications staff on projects Assisting in the coordination of White House and U.S. Capitol tours Drafting and responding to constituent correspondence Interns will gain insight into the legislative process, network with staff, and learn about the inner workings and responsibilities of a congressional office. Interns can expect to work full time and in person Monday through Friday from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM. The internship program will begin on June 1, 2026 and end July 31, 2026. Interested individuals can apply HERE , where they will be able to submit their resume, cover letter, writing sample, and two to three references. Individuals with district ties are strongly encouraged to apply. Applications will be accepted on a rolling basis until positions are filled. The priority deadline to apply is April 1, 2026, but applications will continue to be considered until May 1, 2026 at 5:00 pm CT.

title: Oversight and Investigations Law Clerk
description_text: The House Committee on Energy and Commerce, Subcommittee on Oversight and Investigations (Democratic Staff) is seeking a Law Clerk for Summer 2026 to assist in conducting oversight and investigations of issues within the Committee’s broad jurisdiction. Law Clerks will work closely with Subcommittee counsel and professional staff on a wide range of issues related to the environment, energy, health care, and consumer protection sectors. This is a paid position. Responsibilities include assisting in the preparation for Congressional hearings; contributing to the management of investigations of governmental and private entities; conducting research, document review, and analysis; drafting letters, memoranda, and investigative reports; and coordinating with other Subcommittees of the full Committee. The ideal candidate will be in at least their second year of law school or is a recent law school graduate who is a detail-oriented and proactive problem-solver with strong communication and legal research and writing skills.  Women, people of color, veterans, and LGBTQ+ candidates are strongly encouraged to apply.  The office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, national origin, disability, military status, age, marital status, or parental status. Law clerks will receive a stipend, and applicants may request the Committee’s assistance to receive credit from their educational institution. To apply, please submit a resume, cover letter, and short writing sample to InternshipResume.EC@mail.house.gov with the subject line, “E&C O&I Law Clerk.” Applications are due on March 31, 2026.

title: Communications Aide – Washington, DC
description_text: Congresswoman Sarah McBride (DE-AL) is seeking a highly motivated, creative, and collaborative Communications Aide to join her Washington, DC office. The Communications Aide will primarily manage the office’s constituent mail program while assisting with digital content creation and social media production. The Communications Aide will report directly to the DC-based Communications Director. The ideal candidate will be a self-starter with strong writing, video, and photo editing skills and a creative problem-solver capable of producing high-quality work in a fast- paced environment. A positive attitude, strong attention to detail, the ability to thrive in a collaborative team environment, and a good sense of humor are essential. Previous legislative or communications experience is a plus. Responsibilities include, but are not limited to: ● Executing a robust correspondence program to quickly address constituent requests, concerns, and questions across a range of issues; ● Creating and maintaining a collection of prepared responses for non-casework constituent inquiries, including collaborating with the legislative, outreach, and casework departments to ensure responsive letters are up-to-date; ● Producing high-quality videos and graphics using editing software; ● Drafting Franked Communications, working with the Communications Standards Commission on approval of documents, and sending communications to constituents in mass mailings; ● Assisting with producing social media content, the weekly newsletter, and other materials; ● Capturing content of the Member in Washington, DC, including events and meetings with constituents; ● Staffing the member, including driving, at on- and off-site events; and ● Other special projects as assigned. The ideal candidate will have: ● Excellent writing and verbal communication skills; ● A strong commitment to responding to Delawareans in a timely and thoughtful manner; ●A demonstrated passion for public service; ● Video production and editing skills; ● Familiarity with social media best practices and emerging trends; ● Willingness and ability to work non-traditional hours when required; ● A licensed driver with a clean driving record; and ● Eagerness to learn, grow, and thrive in a dynamic and fast-paced environment. The salary for this position is $55,000 annually with additional benefits like health care, retirement match, 20 days of PTO, 10 days of paid sick leave, and 12 weeks of paid family and medical leave. The office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, national origin, age, disability, military status, marital or parental status. Women, people of color, and other members of traditionally underrepresented communities are strongly encouraged to apply. Delaware ties are a plus. Please send a cover letter, resume, writing sample (2 pages maximum), and 2–3 video/graphic samples in a single PDF to press.mcbride@mail.house.gov with the subject line “ Communications Aide – Last Name .” Applications close March 24, 2026. No calls or walk-ins.

title: Press Assistant
description_text: Press Assistant The Energy and Commerce Committee Democratic Staff seeks a motivated and hardworking communications professional to join fast-paced communications team as a press assistant. Responsibilities include: drafting press releases, social media posts, talking points, and the committee newsletter; designing graphics for both social media and web content; producing and editing videos and photos; monitoring social media; maintaining and updating press lists; and compiling daily press clips. Qualified candidates must have excellent written and oral communication skills, a proven ability to perform under pressure while juggling multiple tasks and sound political judgment. Prior Hill or campaign communications experience, as well as professional social media and digital experience is preferred. Our office deeply values the diversity of our employees and encourages candidates from diverse backgrounds to apply. Salary: $60,000 Qualified candidates should submit a cover letter, resume, one writing sample, and two digital samples to ecdjobs@mail.house.gov. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis with a deadline to apply by March 20, 2026.

title: Legislative Correspondent
description_text: Congresswoman Debbie Wasserman Schultz seeks an organized and motivated self-starter to serve as Legislative Correspondent in her fast-paced Washington, D.C. office . Primary responsibilities include managing the correspondence program, drafting constituent responses, and serving as intern & tour coordinator. Candidates should have excellent oral and written communication skills, attention to detail, and a strong commitment to public service. Primary Responsibilities: Oversee the constituent management system, including sorting incoming messages and timely responses. Research and draft constituent mail with attention to detail to ensure clear and accurate communication across all issue areas. Maintain an organized constituent management system. Process and validate incoming data from multiple sources. Track metrics; produce weekly, quarterly, and annual reports for the mail program and the Legislative Team more broadly. Assist the Legislative Team and the DC Office with administrative tasks and other duties as assigned. Requirements & Qualifications: Exceptional research, writing, and editing skills are essential. Familiarity with IQ is strongly preferred. Previous Hill experience is required. Florida ties are preferred. APPLICATION INSTRUCTIONS : Applicants should submit one PDF that contains a cover letter, résumé and writing sample. Applications should be submitted through this portal. This office is an equal opportunity employer; we do not discriminate based on race, sex, color, age, religion, disability, national origin, uniformed status, sexual orientation, or gender identity or expression. If interested, please apply by Tuesday, March 17, 2026.

title: Press Secretary/Digital Director
description_text: Location: Washington, DC Salary Range: $60-70,000 based on experience Congresswoman Nanette Barragán (CA-44) is seeking a Press Secretary|Digital Director to join our communications team. The Press Secretary|Digital Director will work under the office’s Communications Director to carry out the Congresswoman’s communications and outreach strategy. Ideal candidates are motivated self-starters, detail-oriented, highly organized, possess problem solving skills, thrive in a very fast-paced environment - and be willing to work extended hours and oftentimes under tight time constraints. Key Responsibilities Work with the Communications Director to build and maintain the Congresswoman’s brand. Develop and manage digital content across platforms (Facebook, Instagram, X, Bluesky, and YouTube). Support the office’s social media strategy by drafting social media posts and producing video and graphic materials. Update and manage the official website. Monitor national and local media coverage, as well as social media trends. Clip and caption TV appearances, floor speeches, long and short form videos, and more. Draft and edit press releases, advisories, franked communications, and other communications materials as assigned Help plan and execute press events, interviews, and digital outreach. Maintain press contact lists and support external communications efforts. Qualifications Strong writing, editing, and strategic messaging skills Skilled in Canva, Adobe Suite, or similar platforms Media relations success and ability to assist with communications in a fast-paced environment Los Angeles Metro|Southern California ties are a plus Fluency in Spanish is a plus Must be a collaborative, high-energy team player committed to advancing the office’s mission and responsive to emerging priorities. TO APPLY: Send (1) Cover Letter, (2) Resume, (3) 2 writing samples of 500 words or less, (4) digital/photographic portfolio, and (5) and three references (including name, title, organization, contact details, and your relationship. Pleasesend all materials to CA44Resumes@mail.house.gov With “CA44 Press Secretary” in the Subject Line. The office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, national origin, disability, military status, age, marital status, or parental status.

title: Scheduler/Executive Assistant
description_text: Location: Washington, DC Salary Range: $60,000 - $80,000, depending on experience and qualifications. Main responsibilities include: maintaining the Member’s daily and long-term schedules for both DC and the District; organizing and evaluating all incoming meeting requests and invitations; keeping the Member on schedule throughout the day; working with legislative and communications staff to ensure the Member is prepared for events/meetings; coordinating travel arrangements; processing and managing expenses and reimbursements for the Member; supporting planning and scheduling for the Congressional Hispanic Caucus related to the Chairwoman; Facilitate weekly scheduling meetings with the Member and Chief of Staff to evaluate meeting requests; Coordinating with the Member and Chief of Staff regarding political scheduling requests; Communicating with constituents by phone and email to address requests; Drive the Member as needed; And additional administrative responsibilities as necessary. Candidates must be highly organized, detail-oriented, energetic, and capable of thriving in a fast-paced and rapidly changing workplace. This position requires availability outside of regular business hours including some weekend availability as needed. Previous experience in scheduling/operations and/or Hill experience is preferred. Salary is commensurate with experience. Southern California | Los Angeles Metro area ties, access to a car, and Spanish fluency are a plus. Interested candidates should send a cover letter, resume, and three references with “SCHEDULER” in the subject line to CA44Resumes@mail.house.gov . The office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, national origin, disability, military status, age, marital status, or parental status.

title: Scheduling and Administrative Intern
description_text: The Office of Representative Brad Schneider (D-IL-10) is now accepting applications for a Summer 2026 scheduling & administrative intern in the Washington, D.C. office. This paid internship runs from early June through early August and is open to both undergraduate and graduate students. This internship offers an exciting opportunity to contribute to the Congressman’s success by assisting with administrative tasks, incoming scheduling requests, and constituent services. General hours for this internship are 9:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. when Congress is in session and 9:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. when not in session. Preference will be given to residents of Illinois’ 10th Congressional District, rising Seniors and spring 2026 graduates, and applicants with availability to work three full days or more a week. Key Responsibilities: Serve as the first point of contact for visitors, providing exceptional customer service at the front desk. Answer and direct incoming phone calls, take messages, and handle constituent inquiries efficiently. Assist with managing incoming mail and email, ensuring correspondence is logged and routed appropriately. Organizing, vetting, and tracking incoming meeting requests and invitations; assisting the Director of Scheduling with the Congressman’s daily and long-term schedules; maintaining all scheduling files; and other executive assistant and operations duties as needed. In coordination with the Director of Scheduling, acting as a point of contact for the Congressman’s staff, constituents, and external stakeholders regarding scheduling inquiries. Qualifications: Strong organizational skills with a keen attention to detail and the ability to multitask in a fast-paced environment. Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal, with a professional and friendly demeanor. The ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment and work well with a team. To apply for a scheduling & administrative internship with Congressman Schneider, please complete the internship application form on our website ( https://schneider.house.gov/students/internships ) by 11:59 p.m. EST on March 21, 2026. If you have trouble accessing the application form or have any questions, please email Benjamin.Kaden@mail.house.gov

title: House Democratic Caucus Staff
description_text: The House Democratic Caucus is currently seeking candidates for paid internships for the 2026 summer term. Internships are full-time and in-person in our Washington, DC office. Current college students and recent graduates are welcome to apply. The internship will offer students and young professionals invaluable experience supporting Members and staff of the House Democratic Caucus. Interns contribute to the daily operations of the office and are expected to act in a dependable and professional manner at all times. The ideal candidate will be detail-oriented, work efficiently under pressure and manage multiple projects at one time. The internships include some physical tasks like lifting and pushing carts as part of event setup and logistical responsibilities. Responsibilities will include: Press Intern : Drafting press releases and other written materials, maintaining press lists, and assisting with press events and other Caucus events and meetings. Familiarity in livestreaming and livestreaming software, and fluency in Spanish, is a plus. Digital Intern : Drafting digital copy for the Caucus official social media channels, collaborating on creative graphic and video content, and assisting with press conferences and other Caucus events and meetings. Familiarity with livestreaming and livestreaming software is a plus. Operations Intern : Greeting guests, answering phones, monitoring and responding to the Caucus email inbox, assisting with Caucus Meetings and events, supporting office operations, attending briefings and drafting memos, and various day-to-day office tasks. The House Democratic Caucus is an equal opportunity employer; we do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, disability, military status, parental status, marital status, sexual orientation, or any other factor or basis prohibited by applicable federal law. Applicants with disabilities who require a reasonable accommodation to participate in the application or hiring processes are encouraged to request an accommodation at any time during those processes. Interested applicants should submit an application through this form and upload a resume, cover letter and one-paged writing sample. Digital applicants may submit 1-2 digital samples. The internship will run from Monday, June 1 to Friday, August 10, 2026 . This internship offers compensation of $2,400 per month. The deadline to apply for our fall cohort is Friday, March 27, 2026.

title: Legislative Correspondent
description_text: Congressman Christian Menefee (TX-18) is seeking a Legislative Correspondent to take primary responsibility for drafting responses to constituent outreach on a wide range of policy issues.  Candidates should possess excellent written and oral communication skills, sharp attention to detail, and be a team player with a positive attitude.  The successful candidate will have strong writing skills and the ability to communicate clearly in a fast-paced environment and work effectively with teams in both the DC and District offices. Prior Capitol Hill experience and Texas ties are preferred . Familiarity with IQ, Fireside or comparable constituent relationship management systems strongly preferred. The Legislative Correspondent, reporting directly to the Legislative Director, will serve as an integral member of the Legislative/Policy team. Responsibilities include, but are not limited to, managing a high-volume constituent mail program, researching, and drafting all constituent correspondence, managing legislative team and intern correspondence drafting, maintaining a proactive constituent outreach operation, tracking and reporting on constituent correspondence, and other duties as assigned. The Legislative Correspondent will work closely with the Legislative Director and broader policy team to ensure alignment with the Congressman’s priorities and district needs. Familiarity with the appropriations process is a plus. This is not an entry-level position. The annual salary range for this position is $50,000-$60,000 dependent on experience and qualifications. The position offers health coverage; telework opportunities; retirement benefits with an employer match; paid sick, bereavement, and vacation leave; transit or parking benefits; paid parental leave; paid FMLA leave; access to life insurance coverage; professional development opportunities; and access to the Student Loan Repayment Program. Please note that candidates must be available for occasional weekend and evening work to support the Member when the congressional schedule requires. This is a full-time position. The office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, national origin, age, disability, military status, marital or parental status. Women, people of color, and other members of traditionally underrepresented communities are strongly encouraged to apply. Salary will be based on experience. Those interested in the position should email a resume and cover letter to ResumeTX18@mail.house.gov with the subject line “Legislative Correspondent.” Deadline to apply is March 13th, 2026 , but interviews will be conducted on a rolling basis.

title: Legislative Assistant
description_text: Congressman Christian Menefee (TX-18) , member of the House Space Science and Technology Committee, is seeking a Legislative Assistant position to join his Washington, D.C. team to handle a broad portfolio of policy issues such as healthcare, energy and environment, tribal issues, agriculture, animals, and or other issues as assigned. This is not an entry-level position; prior legislative and/or Capitol Hill experience is preferred. Core responsibilities include: researching and advancing legislative and policy priorities; drafting vote recommendations, memos, and talking points; monitoring legislation and floor activity; preparing the Congressman for briefings and external meetings; and representing the office in meetings with constituents, advocates, and stakeholder groups. The Legislative Assistant will work closely with the Legislative Director and broader policy team to ensure alignment with the Congressman’s priorities and district needs. Texas ties are a plus . Women and minority candidates are encouraged to apply. Strong familiarity with the appropriations process is required. This is not an entry-level position. The annual salary range for this position is $60,000-$80,000 dependent on experience and qualifications. The position offers health coverage; telework opportunities; retirement benefits with an employer match; paid sick, bereavement, and vacation leave; transit or parking benefits; paid parental leave; paid FMLA leave; access to life insurance coverage; professional development opportunities; and access to the Student Loan Repayment Program. Please note that candidates must be available for occasional weekend and evening work to support the Member when the congressional schedule requires. The office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, national origin, age, disability, military status, marital or parental status. Women, people of color, and other members of traditionally underrepresented communities are strongly encouraged to apply. Salary will be based on experience. Those interested in the position should email a resume and cover letter to ResumeTX18@Mail.House.Gov with the subject line “Legislative Assistant.” Deadline to apply is March 13th, 2026 , but interviews will be conducted on a rolling basis.

title: Scheduler
description_text: Congressman Jerrold Nadler seeks a scheduler to manage Member’s daily DC schedule, including organizing and tracking invitations, speaking engagements, and scheduling requests for the Member. Duties also include assisting in coordinating travel arrangements, managing the intern program, maintaining the DC office, and processing flag requests and White House tour requests. Candidates must be highly organized, attentive to detail, and capable of thriving in a rapidly changing environment. Previous scheduling/office management experience is a plus. Congressman Nadler is committed to, and benefits from, a diverse staff, and candidates of all backgrounds are encouraged to apply.  The office does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including marital or parental status, sexual orientation, and gender identity), disability, age, or national origin. Salary range for this position is $60,000 to $70,000 a year. To apply send a resume and cover letter to NY12.JOB@mail.house.gov .

title: Legislative Correspondent
description_text: The Office of U.S. Representative Don Davis (NC-01) is hiring a Legislative Correspondent in our Washington, D.C. office. The ideal candidate will possess exceptional writing and research skills, strong attention to detail and time management, sound judgment, and the ability to prioritize and execute multiple competing responsibilities in a fast-paced environment. This is a writing-intensive position, and the successful candidate must demonstrate the ability to communicate complex policy issues clearly and effectively to constituents and stakeholders. The Legislative Correspondent will work closely with the Deputy Chief of Staff & Legislative Director, the legislative team, and the communications team to manage a high-volume constituent correspondence program and ensure the office’s messaging reflects the priorities and concerns of eastern North Carolina. Responsibilities include managing the office’s constituent correspondence system; interacting with constituents with professionalism and responsiveness; researching and drafting constituent correspondence on a wide range of legislative and policy issues; working with the Staff Assistant to monitor and maintain the constituent correspondence database; and managing intern correspondence assignments. Additional responsibilities include researching legislative issues, coordinating with legislative staff to ensure timely responses on policy matters, supporting proactive constituent outreach efforts across the district, and performing other duties as assigned. Strong writing, research, and organizational skills are required, along with the ability to manage and respond to a high volume of constituent mail on politically sensitive topics in an evenly divided congressional district. Familiarity with or ties to eastern North Carolina or North Carolina’s First Congressional District are a plus but not required. The Office of Representative Davis is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate in employment on the basis of race, religion, sex, national origin, disability, parental status, military service, or any other protected class. To apply, please submit a resume, cover letter, and three writing samples as a single PDF to nc01resumes@gmail.com with “Legislative Correspondent – LAST NAME” in the subject line by March 13, 2026 .

title: Summer 2026 Internship
description_text: Applications are now open for Congressman Cleaver's Summer 2026 D.C. Internship Program. The D.C. program allows interns to gain congressional experience while playing a key role in a legislative office. Intern responsibilities vary daily but typically include sorting constituent mail, answering the phones, attending hearings and briefings, leading tours, drafting constituent response letters, assembling daily press clips, and assisting the legislative staff. Who should apply? College students and recent college graduates are encouraged to apply. Qualified candidates will have strong written and interpersonal skills, a positive work ethic, and the ability to manage multiple tasks at once. The ideal candidate can work a full-time schedule. How do you apply? Applicants should submit a single PDF containing a one-page cover letter with intended start and end dates, a one-page resume, and a writing sample that is no more than 3 pages to MO5internships@mail.house.gov . Please include “DC Summer 2026: [First Name] [Last Name]” as the subject line. Applications for the summer term are due on March 18, 2026, by 11:59 pm. To learn more, please visit our website https://cleaver.house.gov/services/internships . The office is an equal-opportunity employer and does not discriminate based on race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, national origin, disability, military status, age, marital status, or parental status.

title: Summer Legislative Internship
description_text: The Office of Congressman George Whitesides (CA-27) is seeking motivated candidates for a summer internship in the Washington, D.C. office. This internship offers a unique opportunity to observe the inner workings of Capitol Hill and gain insight into the legislative process. Interns will have the opportunity to work with the legislative, communications, and operations teams. Responsibilities include but are not limited to answering constituent phone calls, giving tours of the Capitol, attending policy briefings and meetings, researching legislation for the legislative staff, and other duties as necessary. The summer internship runs from early June through late July/August, with flexible start and end dates. Qualified applicants are friendly, detail-oriented, professional, and possess excellent oral and written communication skills. Ties to CA-27 are a plus. Interested applicants should fill out the form linked here and send a cover letter, resume, and a short (one-page) writing sample. Please NO calls or walk-ins. This application will close on March 20th at 5 pm EST.

title: Communications Intern
description_text: Communications Intern Representative Kevin Mullin (CA-15) is accepting applications for a paid, part-time communications internship in his Washington, DC office for the summer. Applicants should be organized, creative, familiar with current events, and detail oriented. Responsibilities include answering telephones; compiling daily press clips; creating content for social media; drafting press releases, newsletters, and other written materials; maintaining press lists; clipping floor and hearing remarks; and designing graphics. The ideal candidate is team-oriented, has a strong work ethic, and has an ability to excel in fast-paced environments. Excellent written and oral communication skills are a must. Relevant communications skills such as graphic design, social media, and video editing experience are preferred. Internships are in-person and run for roughly 4 months. Interns typically work three days per week, part-time, with flexibility to accommodate class schedules. Interns in the Washington, D.C. office will be compensated at $15/ hour. Applicants will be reviewed on a rolling basis, and are encouraged to apply ASAP . The application will close March 30, 2026. Due to the large volume of applicants, only those who are selected for interviews will be contacted. To apply, please submit a resume, cover letter, and one page writing sample as one PDF, as well as examples of graphics or video you’ve created here , or by visiting our website at https://kevinmullin.house.gov/internships/ Candidates from diverse backgrounds are encouraged to apply. This office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, national origin, disability, military status, age, marital status, or parental status.

title: Legislative Intern
description_text: Legislative Intern Representative Kevin Mullin (CA-15) is accepting applications for part-time internships in his Washington, D.C. & San Mateo district offices for the summer. The ideal candidate will have excellent written and oral communications skills, interest in the legislative process, resourcefulness, and a willingness to learn in a fast-paced environment. Ties to the Bay Area or California are preferred, but not required. Responsibilities include answering phones, researching legislative issues, drafting constituent correspondence, attending policy briefings and hearings, recommending legislation to the Representative, and other tasks as assigned. Internships are in-person and run for roughly 4 months. Interns typically work three days per week, part-time, with flexibility to accommodate class schedules. Interns in the Washington, D.C. office will be compensated at $15/ hour. Interns in both the San Mateo, CA and Washington, D.C. offices are eligible to receive academic credit. Applicants will be reviewed on a rolling basis, and are encouraged to apply ASAP . The application will close March 30, 2026. Due to the large volume of applicants, only those who are selected for interviews will be contacted. Candidates from diverse backgrounds are encouraged to apply. To apply, please submit a resume, cover letter, and one page writing sample as one PDF here , or by visiting our website at https://kevinmullin.house.gov/internships/ This office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, national origin, disability, military status, age, marital status, or parental status.

title: Press Intern
description_text: Press Internship The press intern will work closely with the communications team. Applicants should be organized, creative, familiar with current events, and detail oriented. Responsibilities include answering telephones, leading Capitol tours, compiling daily press clips, creating content for social media, drafting press releases, newsletters, and other written materials, maintaining press lists, clipping floor and hearing remarks; and designing graphics. Ideal candidates have strong writing and communication skills, the ability to manage multiple competing tasks, a strong sense of professionalism, and demonstrate a solid work ethic. Excellent written and oral communication skills are a must. Relevant communications skills such as graphic design, social media, and video editing experience are preferred. District ties are a plus. Diverse and bilingual candidates are strongly encouraged to apply. To Apply: Interested applicants should send a resume, 1-page cover letter, writing sample, and a graphic sample to cisneros.internship@mail.house.gov with "DC Summer 2026 Press Internship Application - First Name Last Name" as the subject line. Please include weekly availability and preferred start/end date on your email and attach the resume and cover letter as a pdf. No calls or drop-ins please.

title: Legislative Intern
description_text: Legislative Internship Legislative interns have the opportunity to learn how the House functions through hands-on experience. Interns will have the opportunity to sit in on committee hearings and briefings, assist with legislative projects, and lead Capitol tours. They will also support the D.C. office by answering phones, greeting visitors, sorting mail, and other administrative duties as assigned, as well as providing support to legislative staff as needed. Ideal candidates have strong writing and verbal communication skills, the ability to manage multiple competing tasks, a strong sense of professionalism, and demonstrate a solid work ethic. District ties are a plus. Diverse and bilingual candidates are strongly encouraged to apply. To Apply: Interested applicants should send a resume and 1-page cover letter to cisneros.internship@mail.house.gov with "DC Summer 2026 Legislative Internship Application - First Name Last Name" as the subject line. Please include weekly availability and preferred start/end date on your email and attach the resume and cover letter as a pdf. No calls or drop-ins please.

title: Communications Director
description_text: The Office of Congressman Christian Menefee (TX-18) is excited to invite applications for a Communications Director to join our Washington, D.C. office. This is an opportunity to help tell the story of a district full of strength, resilience, and possibility; and to help elevate the voices and priorities of the people we serve. In this role, you will help shape and amplify the Congressman’s message while working alongside a team committed to expanding opportunity and delivering results for Texas’ 18th Congressional District. Responsibilities will include: Drafting press releases, statements, talking points, op-eds, and other communications materials Managing media relations, including pitching stories, coordinating interviews, and building relationships with reporters Helping build and guide the office's communications operation, including supervising communications staff Overseeing social media, digital communications, and broader messaging strategy Monitoring media coverage and leading rapid response efforts Advising the Congressman and senior staff on communications strategy and message development We’re looking for someone with strong writing and organizational skills, sound political judgment, and the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment. Experience in communications, journalism, public affairs, or political communications is preferred . Public service is about lifting people up and making sure every community has a voice. This role offers the opportunity to help tell that story every day. If you’re ready to take on the challenge and make a difference, we’d love to hear from you. Please apply using the form here . Texas ties are a plus. The office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, national origin, age, disability, military status, marital or parental status. Women, people of color, and other members of traditionally underrepresented communities are strongly encouraged to apply. Salary will be based on experience. #TeamMenefee is eager to welcome the right candidate to our team as soon as possible. Should you have questions or experience any difficulties applying, please contact Freedom Fox Richardson via email at Freedom.Richardson@Mail.House.Gov

title: Summer 2026 Internship
description_text: The Office of Congresswoman Yassamin Ansari (AZ-03) is seeking motivated candidates for the Summer 2026 internship program in her Washington DC office. Ideal candidates will be organized, detail-oriented, and passionate about public service. Applicants should have excellent communication and writing skills. Key responsibilities include: -          Assisting with front office operations -          Answering phones and processing incoming requests -          Drafting letters, memos, and conducting legislative research -          Compiling daily press clips and tracking news mentions -          Assisting with tours of the Capitol Internships are in-person, paid, and run from May through August, with flexible start and end dates. The application deadline is March 27th. Apply here ! This office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, military status, gender identity, marital status, parental status, or sexual orientation. Applicants with disabilities who require reasonable accommodation to participate in the application or hiring processes are encouraged to request accommodation at any time during those processes.

title: Summer 2026 Congressional Internship
description_text: The Office of Congresswoman Rashida Tlaib (MI-12) is seeking passionate, highly-motivated and energetic candidates to join our team for our Summer 2026 Congressional Internship Program. This internship is open to current college students and recent graduates. Full-time and/or part-time schedules are available. Interested applicants should have a commitment to public service, strong organizational skills, and the ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment. Interns will contribute to the daily operations of each office and are expected to act in a dependable and professional manner at all times. Michigan ties are strongly encouraged, but not required. Intern responsibilities may include, but are not limited to: answering phones, greeting visitors, assisting with Capitol tours, processing constituent correspondence, drafting letters, conducting policy research, and attending congressional hearings and briefings or local district events. The Office of Congresswoman Tlaib is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, disability, age, ethnicity, national origin, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, family status, or military service. Women, people of color, LGBTQ+ individuals, working-class individuals, and members of other underrepresented or marginalized communities are strongly encouraged to apply. To Apply If you are interested in joining our Washington, D.C. Office , please submit your cover letter, resume, and three references in one single PDF titled “ Full Name - DC Summer 2026 Candidate” If you are interested in joining our District Office , please submit your cover letter, resume, and three references in one single PDF titled “ Full Name - District Summer 2026 Candidate” . For consideration, please submit all applications to rashida.internships@mail.house.gov by Friday, March 27th. Applications that are not submitted in the format detailed above will NOT be considered.

title: Director of Operations and Scheduling
description_text: Congresswoman Lateefah Simon, a member of the House Oversight and Small Business committees, seeks a Director of Operations and Scheduling in the Washington, D.C. office. The Director of Operations and Scheduling works closely with the Member, Chief of Staff, and District Director to coordinate large-picture operations and scheduling in the Member's office. This position also works closely with the Chief of Staff (CoS) on the operations budget and plays a key role in coordinating office-wide logistics and technology. Additionally, the Director of Operations and Scheduling acts as a liaison for the office with other Congressional offices, constituents, and other VIPs including executives from the public, private, and non-profit sectors. This position requires long hours, weekends, work across time zones, and limited driving of the Member (access to a vehicle is required). The ideal candidate will have a high attention to detail, excellent verbal and written communication skills, and the ability to remain flexible in a fast-paced environment. This is not an entry level position, with a minimum of at least 2 years’ experience scheduling for a Member of Congress or high-level executive. Strong management skills are required as well. East Bay/California ties are a plus. The Office of Congresswoman Simon is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in employment on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy and gender identity), national origin, political affiliation, sexual orientation, marital status, disability, genetic information, age, membership in an employee organization, retaliation, parental status, military service, or other non-merit factor. Diverse candidates are encouraged to apply. Salary will be based on experience. To apply, please complete this form .  Applications will be considered on a rolling basis. The deadline to apply is March 13, 2026.

title: Staff Assistant/Driver
description_text: Rep. Linda T. Sánchez is seeking a dependable, professional and highly organized individual to serve as a Staff Assistant/Driver in the Washington, D.C. office. Responsibilities include driving the Member to on and off campus events, managing the front office, coordinating tour and flag requests, personal correspondence, working with the scheduling team to coordinate daily briefing materials and travel arrangements, and managing the DC intern program. Additional duties as assigned. Qualified candidates should be detail-oriented, proactive, capable of managing time effectively and prioritizing assignments appropriately. Applicants must be able to exercise discretion and work well in a team environment. The position requires the individual to be available for extended and unscheduled hours (including mornings/nights, weekends and/or holidays). Applicants must possess a valid drivers’ license, impeccable driving skills, and own a registered vehicle. This is an entry level position. Salary: $55,000 – The Office offers a competitive package of benefits (health insurance, retirement match, student loan repayment program, etc.). The Office is an equal opportunity employer and considers all applicants without regard to race, color, national origin, religion, sex, gender identity, disability, or age.  Women and minority candidates are encouraged to apply. Qualified applicants should email a cover letter, resume, and two references with “Staff Assistant/Driver” in the email title to CA38JOBS@mail.house.gov .

title: Press and Digital Fellow
description_text: The Office of Congressman John B. Larson (CT-01) seeks a paid Press and Digital Fellow based in Washington, D.C. to join a fast-paced, collaborative team for Summer 2026. Fellow must be a hard-working individual with demonstrated digital skills and the ability to prioritize multiple tasks at once. Fellowship is full-time for Summer with a monthly stipend. Job Functions: Create timely and engaging social media content Produce high-quality graphics and videos (including script writing and production) Draft press materials like releases and quotes as required Compile daily press clips to be sent to D.C. and District staff Work with Deputy Communications Director and Communications Director to strategize and execute on communications goals All other tasks as assigned Qualifications: Prior experience in digital communications, including graphic design and video editing Strong writing and grammar skills Excellent time management and interpersonal communication skills Junior or Senior year students or new graduates with degrees in communications-focused majors are preferred Please send a cover letter, resume, writing sample (2 pages maximum) and 2-3 graphic or video samples in a single pdf to ct01interns@mail.house.gov with the subject line “Press and Digital Fellow Summer 2026 – Last Name.” Applications will be considered on a rolling basis. We are an equal opportunity employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, sex, color, age, religion, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, or disability. Diverse applicants encouraged to apply. Connecticut ties are a plus.

title: Staff Assistant and Driver
description_text: The Office of Congressman Ted W. Lieu seeks an organized, passionate, team-oriented Staff Assistant and Driver for our Washington DC office. Responsibilities include: managing the front office, coordinating tour and flag requests, assisting the Director of Operations with the internship program, driving the Congressman to and from events during regular office hours and afterhours, and providing additional office support as needed. While this is an entry-level position, candidates should have excellent oral and written communication skills, great attention to detail, strong time management skills, an ability to work under pressure to meet tight deadlines, as well as a good sense of humor! Access to a vehicle in good condition is a plus. Candidate is expected to be on call and available to drive whenever the Congressman is in Washington DC. Please send applications to: Lieu.Staff@mail.house.gov with “Staff Assistant” in the subject line. No calls or drop-ins please. Equal Employment Opportunity Employer. Starting salary is $55,000.

title: Summer 2026 Internship, Washington D.C. or Oakland
description_text: Congresswoman Simon's office hosts internships in both of our offices (Washington D.C. or Oakland). Prospective legislative Intern applicants for the Washington, D.C. office and prospective district office intern applicants are required to fill out the below form and should have their cover letter and resume ready to upload at the end of the form (submitting these documents is required). Prospective press interns for the Washington D.C. office should email their application materials and digital portfolio to lateefahsimonpress@mail.house.gov , following the instructions below. This office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, military status, gender identity, marital status, parental status, or sexual orientation. Applicants with disabilities who require reasonable accommodation to participate in the application or hiring processes are encouraged to request accommodation at any time during those processes. Washington, D.C. Internships (Press and Legislative) Legislative Internship Congresswoman Lateefah Simon (CA-12) seeks paid part-time interns for her Washington, D.C. office for the Summer 2026 semester. Ideal candidates are organized and passionate undergraduate students, graduate students, or recent graduates with an interest in public service and social justice. Applicants should be self-starters with excellent communication, writing and organizational skills. Key responsibilities of D.C. interns include but are not limited to: Assisting with front office operations Conducting Capitol Tours Answering phones, recording constituent opinions, and processing other incoming requests Supporting the legislative team by drafting memos and conducting legislative research or community outreach Attending briefings and congressional hearings Performing other duties as assigned Applications are accepted from Wednesday, March 4th through Sunday, March 22nd at 11:59PM EST. Candidates are encouraged to apply as soon as possible, as applicants will be considered on a rolling basis. The internship will take place in-person during the Summer and is paid. Preference will be given to those with California ties. To apply, please complete the application provided in this form . Press / Communications Internship The Office of Congresswoman Lateefah Simon (CA-12) seeks a paid and highly motivated Communications Intern for Summer 2026 (June-July) based in the Washington, D.C. office. The Communications Intern works closely with the Communications Team to support a robust press and digital operation. Ideal candidates are organized and passionate undergraduate students, graduate students, or recent graduates with an interest in public service and social justice. Applicants must be able to work in a fast-paced environment and should have excellent writing and organizational skills and a keen attention to detail. Applicants should have graphic design and video editing skills. Primary educational opportunities and responsibilities will include: Drafting engaging and creative social media content and graphics highlighting Member’s work; Clipping committee remarks and press (cable, radio, other media) hits; Compiling daily news clips and maintaining internal clips lists; Drafting press releases, video scripts, and other written materials; Maintaining press lists; Media monitoring and research; Other communications projects as they arise. Applications will be accepted from Wednesday March 4th through Sunday, March 22nd at 11:59PM EST. Candidates are encouraged to apply as soon as possible, as applicants will be considered on a rolling basis. The internship will take place in-person in Washington D.C. during the summer and is paid. Preference will be given to those with California ties. To apply, please submit a resume, writing sample, and short digital portfolio to lateefahsimonpress@mail.house.gov . Please send your short digital portfolio as attachments or a shareable google drive link. Oakland, CA Internship Congresswoman Lateefah Simon (CA-12) seeks three (3) paid part-time interns for her Oakland District Office for Summer 2026. Interns will work in-person, 24 hours per week. Ideal intern candidates are organized and passionate undergraduate students, graduate students, or recent graduates with an interest in government, constituent services, and social justice. Key responsibilities of District Interns include, but are not limited to: Assisting with answering constituent inquiries, front-office operations; Answering phones, recording constituent correspondences, and processing other incoming requests; Drafting memos; Beginning Constituent Casework support; Community and District engagements, including tabling and drafting government and constituent resources; Applications will be accepted from Wednesday March 4th through Sunday, March 22nd at 11:59PM EST. Selected candidates must not be working or interning in another Legislative or Federal department at the same time. The Summer 2026 Internship Program will run from June 8th to August 21st. Preference will be given to those with ties to California's 12th District. To apply, please complete the application provided in this form .

title: Press Assistant/Digital Assistant
description_text: The office of Congressman Herb Conaway Jr., M.D., is currently seeking a Press Assistant/Digital Assistant for the Washington, D.C., office. Responsibilities include facilitating the day-to-day operations of an active press operation for a new House Democrat, drafting and creating innovative content for the member’s social media platforms, designing graphics and videos, fielding and executing video requests, photographing events, overseeing a press intern, and performing other duties as assigned. Key responsibilities include: Assisting the Communications Director in creating a proactive digital plan to highlight his district, legislative, and constituent service work, including strategies for growth across platforms. Draft and edit releases, statements, talking points, newsletters, captions, and quotes – including rapid response to breaking news. Record, edit, and caption short-form videos for social media, press events, and constituent communications. Work collaboratively with the district office to ensure a strong local-first focus in written and digital products. Monitor and compile daily press clips, traditional media coverage, and social media metrics. Staff, photograph, and film the Congressman at scheduled events and meetings. Support the Communications Director and staff on other assignments as needed. Suggest new approaches and pitch opportunities for traditional and digital communications Preferred qualifications include: Familiarity with various digital platforms, maintaining websites, and managing social media accounts for a political candidate, elected official, or organization. Excellent written and verbal communication skills. Proficient in graphic design and video editing. Self-starter who is able to work in a fast-paced environment. Some experience with franked communications preferred but not required. New Jersey ties are a plus. If you wish to apply, please submit your application (resume, cover letter, one digital sample, and two writing samples) to Resume.herbconaway@mail.house.gov with "Press Assistant/Digital Assistant" in the subject line.

title: Staff Assistant/Legislative Correspondent/Driver
description_text: The Office of U.S. Representative Pramila Jayapal (WA-07) seeks a highly organized, passionate, team-oriented Staff Assistant/Legislative Correspondent/ and Driver in her Washington, D.C. office. Responsibilities include but are not limited to: Executing a robust correspondence program to quickly address constituent requests, concerns, and questions; Driving the Congresswoman to and from events during regular office hours and when needed, after hours; Managing front office operations, greeting visitors and organizing tours and other constituent services; Supporting the Chief of Staff and Deputy Chief of Staff with personnel, technology, and financial management duties; Providing administrative support across the D.C. and District offices, Managing the Washington, D.C. internship and fellows program; and Other special projects as required. The ideal candidate will be: Willing and able to often work irregular hours; Passionate about public service and committed to progressive values; An expert with spreadsheets, numbers, and forms, with strong attention to detail; Excellent writing and verbal communication skills; A licensed driver with a clean driving record and a four-door car in good condition; Eager to learn, grow, and thrive in a dynamic and high-paced environment. Compensation : Salary is $60,000. The position offers health coverage; retirement benefits with an employer match; paid sick, bereavement, and vacation leave; transit or parking benefits; access to life insurance coverage; and access to the Student Loan Repayment Program. The Office of Congresswoman Pramila Jayapal is an equal opportunity employer. Women, people of color, LGBTQ+ individuals, new Americans, people with disabilities, and members of other underrepresented communities are strongly encouraged to apply. State and/or district ties are a plus. Qualified candidates should include in their application: resume, cover letter, and references. Please send applications to jobs.pramila@mail.house.gov with “Staff Assistant” in the subject line. No calls or drop-ins please.

title: Legislative Director
description_text: Congresswoman Lateefah Simon, a member of the House Oversight and Small Business committees, seeks a Legislative Director to lead the office’s legislative operation and oversee the development and execution of her policy agenda. The Legislative Director plays an essential leadership role in the office by working closely with the Chief of Staff in assisting with the formulation of the Member’s policy and legislative strategy. The Legislative Director will also work with the Communications Director to ensure that the Member’s legislative strategy is incorporated into the office’s communications plans. The Legislative Director is also responsible for overseeing the legislative team, which currently consists of two legislative assistants and one legislative correspondent.  The LD will be responsible for overseeing the office’s appropriations process, and engaging in community outreach to local stakeholders along with the District Director on the Congresswoman’s agenda. This position will also work closely with the District Director and Chief of Staff to ensure community project funding priorities are included and reflected throughout the appropriations process. The LD will also maintain a policy portfolio and is the lead staffer responsible for identifying and advancing legislative opportunities for the Congresswoman, including drafting legislation, amendments and opportunities to speak on the House Floor on the Congresswoman’s priorities. This position requires long hours, weekends, travel to the district, along with strong leadership and deep knowledge of the legislative process.  This is not an entry level position, with a minimum of at least 6 years’ experience a legislative staffer. Strong management skills are required as well. East Bay/California ties are a plus but not required. The Office of Congresswoman Simon is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in employment on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy and gender identity), national origin, political affiliation, sexual orientation, marital status, disability, genetic information, age, membership in an employee organization, retaliation, parental status, military service, or other non-merit factor. Diverse candidates are encouraged to apply. Salary will be based on experience. To apply, please complete this form .  Applications will be considered on a rolling basis. The deadline to apply is Friday, March 13, 2026.

title: Communications Director
description_text: Congresswoman Joyce Beatty (OH-03) seeks an innovative communications leader who can design, produce, and turn around products that respond to the moment. Ability to anticipate problems and offer solutions, and familiarity with Ohio and national news outlets are key. This position is based in D.C., with occasional travel to Columbus during District work periods, and reports to the Chief of Staff. The ideal candidate thrives in fast-paced environments, is comfortable working under deadlines, is confident interacting with reporters, is highly collaborative and eager to work with staff, and has a passion for politics and video production. ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS: Develops and implements media, communications, public relations, and social media strategies; Remains abreast of current legislative and non-legislative issues; Proactively drafts remarks, talking points, and other content in the voice of the Congresswoman each day, in addition to quickly responding to breaking national and district-local news; Manages the Member’s official website; Drafts regular commentary and op-eds for news outlets on important subject matter and current events; Creates videos, graphics, and other digital content that is compelling, engaging, on-brand, and error-free; Develops and executes press conferences and public events; Oversees the creation and distribution of the Congresswoman’s newsletters and press releases, and produces franked communications; Maintains a good working relationship with the Congresswoman, staff, media, and constituents; Supervises and mentors press staff, interns, and district communications support; Works well under pressure and manages stress well; Accepts performance-based criticism and direction; Works a flexible schedule, including long hours, evenings and weekends when needed; and Performs other duties as assigned. EDUCATION/EXPERIENCE: This is not an entry level position. A bachelor’s degree and 5+ years of professional communications experience is required. District ties and a thorough understanding of the Columbus media landscape are preferred but not required. Salary & Benefits: Compensation is commensurate with experience, in line with House salary ranges. The office provides a robust benefits package.  The position is eligible for transit benefits, student loan repayment assistance, and parking. This is an in-person position with remote work opportunities. QUALIFICATIONS REQUIRED: Strong writing, editing, and proofreading skills; Strong communication skills; Understanding of local and national news outlets; Thorough   knowledge   of    the    legislative    process,    procedures   and organization of the House; and Knowledge of video and graphic editing software and IQ Applicants should send a resume, cover letter, writing sample, example(s) of graphic or video product(s), and three professional references in one PDF to OH3.Jobs@mail.house.gov with the subject line "Communications Director – Name." Applications will be considered on a rolling basis. No phone calls, faxes, or separate emails please.

title: Summer 2026 Intern
description_text: The Office of Congresswoman Eleanor Holmes Norton provides seasonal internship opportunities to undergraduate and graduate students interested in gaining congressional work experience in her Capitol Hill Office and in her District Office. Responsibilities of congressional interns include, but are not limited to, answering telephones, opening and sorting mail, organizing Capitol tours to constituents, tracking legislation, attending community events, assisting with constituent casework, coordinating press-related activities, attending hearings, and preparing constituent correspondence. These tasks give students valuable, direct interaction with the inner workings of Capitol Hill and the legislative process. I f you would like to be considered for an internship in Congresswoman Norton's Capitol Hill office , please include the following as individual attachments : one-page cover letter, resume, writing sample (2 pages max.) and at least one letter of recommendation in an email to Raya.Kenney@mail.house.gov with the following in the subject line: Norton Hill Office Summer 2026 Internship Application - First Name Last Name To be considered for an internship in Congresswoman Norton's District Office , please include the following as individual attachments : one-page cover letter, resume, writing sample (2 pages max.) and at least one letter of recommendation in an email to dc00.nortonresume@mail.house.gov with the following in the subject line: Norton District Office Summer 2026 Internship Application  - First Name Last Name If you are applying for both offices, please do so in separate emails. **Please note that internships are in person, require a minimum three-month commitment, are paid, and require applicants to be enrolled in or recently graduated from a post-secondary institution. DC ties are preferred, but not required. Candidates from diverse backgrounds are encouraged to apply. The office is an equal opportunity employer; we do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, gender identity or expression, or sexual orientation. No phone calls or drop-ins please.

title: Press and Digital Assistant
description_text: Congresswoman Laura Gillen (NY-04) is seeking a Press/ Digital Assistant to support the office’s earned, paid franking, and social media strategies. This position is based in the Washington, D.C. office. The ideal candidate will possess strong writing skills, experience with video and graphic design, an interest in traditional and digital media strategy, and a good sense of humor. Attention to detail is a must. Ties to Long Island are a plus, but not required. Responsibilities: Craft engaging social media content in the Congresswoman’s voice to promote the member’s priorities and respond to breaking news. Assist with drafting and distributing press releases, advisories, constituent newsletters, statements, and speeches. Work with the Communications Director in identifying new ways to engage with members of the press and online audiences. Take high-quality photos and videos of the Congresswoman for compelling content. Monitor and compile daily news clips mentioning the Congresswoman. Maintain the official website and press list. Preferred qualifications: Outstanding written and digital communications skills. Experience with the Adobe Suite, Canva, or other content creation tools. Ability to manage multiple tasks and think critically in a deadline-driven environment. Commitment to understanding the Member’s unique voice and messaging. Strong visual intelligence and photography skills. Sharp attention to detail. This position will sometimes require evening and weekend work and will report to the Communications Director. To apply, please email NY04Press@mail.house.gov with “Press/ Digital Assistant - [Your Name]” in the subject line and the following application materials attached as a single combined PDF: Resume Two writing samples Three social media content samples The salary range for this position is $55,000-$60,000, commensurate with experience. The office is an equal opportunity employer; we do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, national origin, disability, military status, age, marital status, or parental status. Candidates of diverse backgrounds are encouraged to apply.

title: Director of Communications
description_text: The Office of Congressman Steven Horsford seeks an experienced and strategic Director of Communications to lead a high-volume, high-impact communications operation. The Director of Communications leads the development and execution of the office’s communications strategy, working closely across the team to shape and roll out messaging around legislation, major announcements, and public engagements. The Director serves as the Member’s media liaison, secures and manages local and national press opportunities, prepares and staffs the Member for interviews and events, and oversees all press, digital, and public-facing communications. The Director directly manages two highly skilled communications staff members and is responsible for setting clear priorities, workflows, and performance expectations to ensure a disciplined, high-functioning communications operation. The ideal candidate is strategic, steady, and composed, with the ability to operate calmly in fast-paced, high-pressure environments.
