They are confident, professional, and mission-driven, with the judgment to enforce message discipline and the confidence to say “no” to ideas that are off-strategy. The ideal candidate is receptive to feedback, responsive to performance-based direction, and committed to continuous improvement and team accountability. Key Responsibilities 1. Message Strategy & Narrative Control The Director of Communications translates long-term strategy into weekly guidance, works closely with legislative staff on media strategies for bill rollouts and major announcements, anticipates emerging narratives, and enforces message discipline across all platforms. 2. Earned Media Management The Director of Communications secures and prepares the Congressman for national and local television, radio, print, and digital media, maintains a proactive pitch calendar aligned with legislative and district priorities, and builds strong, trusted relationships with reporters, producers, editors, and bookers. 3. Digital & Platform Oversight The Director of Communications oversees digital staff and external vendors, ensures alignment between press strategy and digital amplification, and supervises the creation of official digital ads, graphics, and multimedia assets. 4. Rapid Response & Contrast The Director of Communications drafts statements, talking points, op-eds, speeches, etc. in the Congressman’s voice, coordinates closely with Member offices, caucus communications teams, and allies, and provides strategic counsel on the media implications of legislative actions while making sound, split-second decisions under tight deadlines. 5. Events, Briefings & Member Preparation The Director of Communications plans and staffs press conferences, tele-town halls, roundtables, and major DC- and district-based events; prepares and briefs the Congressman for interviews and public engagements; ensures readiness on legislative and non-legislative issues; and maintains working knowledge of House rules, franking regulations, and compliance requirements. 6. Team Management The Director of Communications directly supervises the Director of Digital Media and the Communications Specialist, and establishes clear workflows, authority, and accountability across the communications function. Required Qualifications Experience This is not an entry-level position. Previous communications experience is required. Previous Hill experience is a plus. Demonstrated success securing and managing national media coverage. Experience integrating press, digital, events, and legislative communications. Experience managing staff. Familiarity with House franking rules and compliance requirements. Core Skills Exceptional writing, editing, and proofreading skills. Deep understanding of print, broadcast, and online media. Strong political instincts and strategic judgment. Experience with digital communications, including paid ads and analytics. Ability to work calmly and decisively under pressure. Equal Opportunity Employer This office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, national origin, sex, gender identity or expression, pregnancy, sexual orientation, age, disability, marital status, parental status, military status, or any other status protected by federal law. Application Instructions Qualified candidates should submit a resume and cover letter demonstrating relevant experience and interest in the role. All materials should be sent to the office’s Chief of Staff, Yvanna Cancela at yvanna.cancela@mail.house.gov .

title: DC Summer Internship
description_text: Congresswoman Rosa L. DeLauro (CT-03) will begin seeking applicants for the Summer 2026 internship program in her Washington, D.C. Office on March 2nd. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis. Responsibilities: The office of Congresswoman DeLauro runs an internship program intended for current undergraduate students, recent college graduates, and students currently enrolled in graduate school. Internships in Congresswoman DeLauro's office combine administrative and legislative work. Interns are involved in all aspects of the office including constituent services, correspondence, and legislative research, in addition to providing general staff support. The Washington internship program also offers an opportunity for a press internship. This position will assist the communications team with social media, press conferences, and more. To apply for this position, please check the press internship interest box in the program application. Press Intern tasks include, but are not limited to: Drafting for social media Compiling daily press clips Clipping and editing videos Office administrative tasks Other tasks as assigned by the Communications team Qualifications: Applicants should have an ability to work in a fast-paced environment, possess excellent writing skills, and an enthusiastic attitude. Interning in Congresswoman DeLauro's office is a great opportunity to work in a highly active legislative office. How to Apply: Please email your completed intern application form (located on delauro.house.gov/services/internships), cover letter, resume, brief writing sample (1-2 pages), and two reference contacts in a single PDF file to Eamon.Moylan@mail.house.gov with the subject line “First and Last Name, Summer 2026 Internship” by March 23rd at 5pm .

title: Legislative Assistant
description_text: Congressman Steven Horsford (NV-04) seeks an experienced full-time Legislative Assistant for his Washington, D.C. office. The Legislative Assistant will manage the Congressman’s portfolio for the House Ways and Means Subcommittees on Work and Family Support and on Social Security, as well as issues related to immigration, homeland security, judiciary, veterans, humanities, and other assigned policy areas. Prior legislative and/or Capitol Hill experience is required . Job Duties: Collaborates with the Legislative Director to develop and advance legislative initiatives within assigned issue areas; Tracks legislation, hearings, regulatory actions, and policy developments; prepare briefings for floor activity, committee work, district engagements, and external events; Represents the Congressman at meetings with constituents, advocacy organizations, stakeholders, and senior government officials; Coordinates with district office staff to support community events, town halls, and local stakeholder engagement; Partners with communications staff to contribute to speeches, press releases, op-eds, talking points, and digital content Serves as liaison to committees, federal departments, and agencies to advance legislative priorities Acts as a liaison with committee and Federal departments and agencies to communicate and coordinate information regarding specific legislative matters Prepares comprehensive briefing materials for hearings, markups, meetings, and votes Drafts policy memos, legislative language, vote recommendations, and talking points Additional duties as assigned by the Legislative Director, Director of Operations, and Chief of Staff Requirements: Prior legislative and/or Capitol Hill experience required Strong understanding of the legislative process, congressional procedure, and federal regulatory frameworks Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to produce clear, persuasive materials for diverse audiences Demonstrated attention to detail and commitment to high-quality, accurate work Proven ability to anticipate needs and deliver results in a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment Skilled relationship-builder with the ability to collaborate effectively across teams and with external partners Flexible, adaptable, and responsive to evolving priorities Demonstrated commitment to continuous professional growth Ability to foster teamwork, cooperation, and a positive office culture A well-developed equity lens and a commitment to continuous learning and growth Demonstrated capacity for strong attention to detail, ensuring all work is thorough, accurate, and completed with care Bonuses: Ties to Nevada Demonstrated commitment to public service The salary range for this position is $60,000 to $75,000 annually, commensurate with experience, and includes health, vision, and dental insurance, paid vacation days and sick time, student loan repayment, and transit benefits. Interested candidates should submit a resume, cover letter, and a one-to-two-page writing sample via this form . This office is an equal opportunity employer; we do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, disability, military status, parental status, marital status, sexual orientation, or any other factor or basis prohibited by applicable federal law.  Applicants with disabilities who require reasonable accommodation to participate in the application or hiring processes are encouraged to request an accommodation at any time during those processes.

title: Field Representative/Grants Specialist
description_text: The Resident Commissioner of Puerto Rico, Pablo José Hernández, seeks a Field Representative/Grants Specialist based in his Caguas, Puerto Rico office. This is not a remote position. In close collaboration with the District Director, Senior Field Representatives, and other District Staff, the Field Representative/Grants Specialist will act as a liaison to federal, state, and local government entities and agencies on behalf of the Resident Commissioner’s constituents. This individual will also oversee federal grant-related activities, serving as the primary point of contact for constituents, local governments, nonprofits, educational institutions, and community organizations seeking federal funding. This position reports to the District Director and is not an entry-level position. Responsibilities include preparing and managing incoming and outgoing casework in assigned areas of expertise; providing technical assistance to constituents seeking federal grants; tracking Notices of Funding Opportunity (NOFOs) and relevant program updates; coordinating letters of support and other congressional documentation as appropriate; and serving as a representative for the Member at District events. Candidates should be fully fluent in Spanish and English languages, have a minimum of 3-5 years of similar experience, and strong writing and organizational skills. The ideal candidate is collaborative, energetic, detail-oriented, able to multi-task, and committed to public service while working in a fast-paced and demanding setting. Puerto Rico ties, extensive knowledge of Puerto Rican issues, and prior government experience are strongly preferred. To apply, send in a single PDF a resume, cover letter, and writing sample to HernandezJobs@mail.house.gov with the following subject line: “Full Name – Field Representative/Grants Specialist” by Friday, March 13, 2026 . Interviews will be held on a rolling basis. The office is an equal opportunity employer and encourages candidates from all backgrounds to apply.

title: Digital Manager + Press Secretary
description_text: The Digital Manager + Press Secretary will work closely with the Communications Director, Chief of Staff, Legislative Director, and District Director to develop and execute an integrated communications strategy that advances legislative priorities, elevates the Member’s profile, and engages constituents. Key Responsibilities Include: Leading digital strategy and content across X, Instagram, Facebook, Substack, Bluesky, email, and the website Drafting and distributing the weekly newsletter Translating legislative and district work into compelling digital storytelling Tracking digital performance metrics and recommending growth strategies Fielding and responding to media inquiries Building relationships with national, regional, trade, and new media outlets Supporting proactive media strategy and rapid response Staffing DC-based events (press conferences, interviews, podcasts, etc.) Assisting with media prep, message development, and crisis communications Qualifications: Excellent written and verbal communication skills Strong digital media expertise Video editing and graphic design skills Media relations experience Sound political judgment and knowledge of the legislative process Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment Hill and/or media experience preferred. Bachelor’s degree preferred. Salary: $68,000–$72,000 annually Comprehensive federal benefits included. To Apply: Email jobs.landsman@mail.house.gov with the subject line: “Your Name - Press Secretary + Digital Manager” (Example: Jane Smith - Press Secretary + Digital Director) Please submit one combined PDF including: Cover letter (including why you want to work in a frontline Member office) Resume Writing sample Sample graphic or design piece Video editing sample (Google or YouTube link preferred) Candidates of diverse backgrounds are encouraged to apply. The office is an equal-opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, military status, age, marital status, or parental status.

title: Summer District (OR) Internship with the Office of Congresswoman Bynum
description_text: Summer District (OR) Internship with the Office of Congresswoman Bynum The Office of Congresswoman Janelle Bynum (OR-05) is seeking full-time and part-time interns to join our office in Oregon City. The preferred internship will run from May 2026 to mid-late August 2026. Seeking applicants who are motivated to learn more about the legislative process, grow professionally, and engage thoughtfully with staff, constituents, and other lawmakers. DUTIES: Handle incoming calls with poise, professionalism, and promptness; Assist Member and key staff with logistical and administrative duties; Receive and log input from constituents on policy matters for the Member office’s consideration and response; Organize press clips and assist the communications team with capturing content and producing talking points; Develop special projects related to your specific field of interests; Maintaining the integrity of the office. QUALIFICATIONS: College student in good academic standing; Good oral and written communication skills; The ability to handle difficult situations with courtesy, tact and compassion Problem-solving skills and the ability to work independently and use good judgement; Responsible, dependable and willing to learn. HOW TO APPLY: Please fill out the internship application on our website linked here. Please select “Summer” to the question regarding “Desired Session.” Applicants are encouraged to apply by March 13th, 2026. NOTICE: All applicants will be considered without regard to race, color, religion, sex (including marital or parental status), sexual orientation, gender identity, disability, age, or national origin.

title: Legislative Assistant or Senior Legislative Assistant
description_text: Congresswoman Brittany Pettersen (CO-07) seeks an experienced Legislative Assistant or Senior Legislative Assistant to cover a legislative portfolio including health, childcare, judiciary, labor, and other issues for the office. The LA will work closely with the Legislative Director and Chief of Staff to develop and execute a coordinated legislative strategy for the Member. The position is full time with federal benefits, including student loan repayment program (if eligible), transit or parking benefits, and an annual salary between $60k-$75k, salary and title commensurate with experience. JOB RESPONSIBILITIES: The ideal candidate is an excellent writer, self-starter, and is collaborative and well organized. The Legislative Assistant will work closely with the Member and Legislative Director in developing legislation, working with stakeholders, preparing remarks, providing vote recommendations, staffing meetings, writing letters, and other duties as assigned. APPLICATION INSTRUCTIONS: This is a full-time, benefits-eligible position based in Washington, D.C. Interested applicants should send a single PDF including a cover letter, resume, two writing samples, to pettersen.resumes@teampettersen.com with the subject line: Last Name – LA Application . Applicants will be considered on a rolling basis. Candidates from diverse backgrounds and underrepresented communities are strongly encouraged to apply. The U.S. House of Representatives is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, military status, gender identity and expression, marital status, parental status, or sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law, in any of its activities or operations.

title: Legislative Assistant
description_text: The Office of Representative Jim Himes (CT-04) is seeking candidates to apply for an open Legislative Assistant position to handle a broad policy portfolio including transportation, infrastructure, energy, environment, agriculture, health care, and seniors among others. This position will report to the Chief of Staff and Legislative Director. Qualified candidates will be independent self-starters with a demonstrated ability to generate new ideas and work well as part of a team. They will also have strong writing, research, analytical, and organizational skills. Candidates should have at least two years of policy experience. This policy position will be responsible for: Advancing the Member’s priorities in the assigned issue areas; Monitoring pending legislation and current events in assigned issue areas; Recommending and executing legislative strategies within assigned issue areas and identifying leadership opportunities for the Member; Reviewing requests for the Member to cosign letters, cosponsor legislation, or attend events; Meeting with constituents and outside groups on behalf of the Member; Working with the district staff to advance key projects in CT-04; Preparing briefing materials and talking points for the Member; Staffing the member at events and meetings on topics within the assigned issue areas; Reviewing materials prepared by the communications team on topics within assigned issue areas, including constituent letters, social media posts, and press releases; Assisting with administrative responsibilities as needed, including responding to constituent outreach; The office is an equal opportunity employer and encourages people of all backgrounds to apply. Applicants with Connecticut ties are encouraged to apply. Interested applicants should send a cover letter, resume, and writing sample to: CT04.Jobs@mail.house.gov with “Legislative Assistant or Legislative Assistant - [NAME]” in the subject line. Applications will be considered on a rolling basis. The last day to submit an application is March 6, 2026.

title: D.C. Scheduler
description_text: Job Description D.C. Scheduler for D.C. office OFFICE of CONGRESSWOMAN ANDREA SALINAS SUMMARY: Congresswoman Andrea Salinas (D-OR) is seeking a D.C. Scheduler in her Washington, D.C. Office. Candidates must have exceptional organizational and communications skills, be very detail oriented, and enjoy working in a fast-paced team environment. The ideal candidate will have proven knowledge of the congressional work setting, ability to balance competing demands, possess sound political judgement and must be able to exercise discretion and have a keen eye for detail. The position requires a willingness to work non-traditional hours when required. The D.C. scheduler will work closely with the Chief of Staff, Legislative Director, and Communications Director to ensure all scheduling needs are met. Successful candidates will develop in-depth knowledge of the district’s geography and key stakeholders in order to meet strategic goals and priorities. ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS: ●     Managing all incoming scheduling requests, collecting relevant logistical and policy information, and presenting to the leadership team; ●     Updating the Congresswoman’s calendar while balancing core office and personal priorities; ●     Coordinate weekly briefing book and schedule, working with the D.C. staff to ensure all briefing materials arrive to the Congresswoman complete and on time; ●     Maintains and implements administrative office files, policies, and operating procedures to ensure effective office operations; ●     Update the staff and Congresswoman of any scheduling changes throughout the workday; ●     Arranging all travel for the Congresswoman; ●     Handles and coordinates logistics for external room reservations, catering, and serves as the main billing point of contact, tracking receipts, and payments; ●     Oversees coordination with other Member offices, Committees, the White House and Executive Departments and agencies – e.g., Travel Logistics –Planning and Press/Speaking Engagements; ●     Tracks office technology (cell phones, iPads, laptops, etc.) and office equipment in the D.C. Office, ensuring the office inventory list is up to date; ●     Maintains the Member's files, including notes, correspondence, contact information for Members and office VIPs, and all information relating to travel; ●     Collecting and processing office expenses; ●     Lead and coordinate all logistical planning calls with staff for the Congresswoman’s travels; ●     Acts as the Member’s primary driver while the Member is in the Washington, D.C. metro area. This is a full-time position based in Washington, D.C. that often requires availability on nights and weekends to support the Member’s movements in and around Washington. The D.C. Scheduler will develop and maintain a collaborative relationship with the District Operations Director. Some hybrid work may be available during non-session weeks. Capitol Hill or other relevant administrative, operations, or scheduling experience is strongly preferred. Reliable access to a car to drive the Member and a clean driving record are required. This Office is an equal opportunity employer; we do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. All qualified individuals are strongly encouraged to apply. The salary range is $73,000-$77,000. Candidates should send their resume and cover letter to: OR06resumes@mail.house.gov with DC Scheduler in the subject line. Applications will be accepted until March 20.

title: Scheduler
description_text: The Office of Congressman Bill Foster seeks an organized, detail-oriented, and responsible individual with previous scheduling experience to serve as Scheduler in our Washington, D.C. office. Responsibilities include managing and maintaining the Congressman’s daily and long-term schedule in D.C, organizing and tracking invitations, arranging travel for the Representative and for staff traveling on official business, keeping the Congressman on schedule throughout the day, collecting and processing the Member’s expenses, compiling necessary materials for the Member’s engagements and performing other duties as required. This is not an entry level position; it requires someone who is able to be on call and available to assist the Member after regular business hours. Capitol Hill and/or executive assistant experience is preferred. The ideal candidate would be detail-oriented, able to juggle multiple tasks at once, have excellent interpersonal skills, be a problem solver and be able to handle last minute changes to the Member’s schedule. Salary range is $60,000 - $75,000 annually commensurate with experience. To apply please send a resume and a cover letter to IL11.Jobs@mail.house.gov with “Scheduler – Your Name” in the subject line. No calls or drop-ins please. Diverse candidates, women, and LGBTQ+ persons are strongly encouraged to apply. Illinois or Midwest ties a plus. Deadline to apply is March 13, 2026.

title: Digital and Creative Director
description_text: The Congressional Progressive Caucus (CPC) seeks a Digital and Creative Director . The person in this role will generate and execute creative ways to showcase the CPC’s work and get our message out, especially on the Internet. As the first ever CPC Digital and Creative Director, the person in this role will: Generate creative ideas for new ways to get the caucus’ message out to larger audiences on the Internet; Maintain CPC’s Twitter and Instagram profile and explore expanding to more social platforms, with an emphasis on vertical video platforms; Create videos, graphics, and text posts showcasing the CPC’s message and members; Analyze data about our creative content and identify opportunities to improve; Learn about CPC policy priorities and messaging and distill complicated topics into content that can break through; Oversee the organization’s visual and brand standards; Support individual CPC offices with trainings and coaching on digital work; Mentor and help supervise a Digital Fellow; Manage the CPC website; Other tasks to support Caucus work as assigned. Key skills will include: Creativity and ability to be a self-starter who generates ideas that drives forward the caucus’ message; The ability to churn out quick rapid response content that responds to the news while also working on medium to long term videos; Ability to oversee video and graphics projects at every stage from ideation to shooting to editing; Ability to build relationships and collaborate across a wide variety of offices and organizations to get results; Interest in progressive politics. Candidates should submit a brief cover letter, resume, and two work samples to ProgressiveCaucusJobs@gmail.com with “Creative Director” in the subject line. Please include at least one video work sample. In your cover letter, please specify what design and editing software you know how to use. Applications will be considered on a rolling basis, so applicants are encouraged to apply as soon as possible. The CPC is an equal-opportunity employer; we do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, gender identity, or expression, or sexual orientation. The salary range for this position is $70,000 – $80,000 dependent on experience.

title: Summer 2026 DC Internship
description_text: Summer D.C. Internship with the Office of Congresswoman Bynum The Office of Congresswoman Janelle Bynum (OR-05) is seeking full-time and part-time interns to join our office in Washington DC. The preferred internship will run from May 2026 to mid-late August 2026. Seeking applicants who are motivated to learn more about the legislative process, grow professionally, and engage thoughtfully with staff, constituents, and other lawmakers. DUTIES: Handle incoming correspondence with poise, professionalism, and promptness; Assist Member and key staff with logistical and administrative duties; Update the office’s legislative tracker and perform research on legislative measures; Attend briefings and hearings, conduct research, and draft correspondence and memoranda; Partner with communications team to create speeches, social media posts, and talking points. QUALIFICATIONS: College student in good academic standing; Strong oral and written communication skills; The ability to handle difficult situations with courtesy, tact and compassion; Problem-solving skills and the ability to work independently and use good judgement; Responsible, dependable and willing to learn. HOW TO APPLY: Please fill out the internship application on our website linked here. Please select “Summer” to the question regarding “Desired Session.” Applicants are encouraged to apply by March 13th, 2026. NOTICE: All applicants will be considered without regard to race, color, religion, sex (including marital or parental status), sexual orientation, gender identity, disability, age, or national origin.

title: Summer 2026 DC Internship
description_text: The office of Congresswoman Susie Lee is seeking full-time, Summer 2026 Interns for the Washington, D.C. office. Responsibilities will include answering phones, managing the front office, assisting legislative staff with research, assisting the communications teams with drafting, and other tasks as assigned. Successful candidates work well under deadlines, are organized, hard-working, detail-oriented, have excellent customer service skills, and are team players. Applicants with Nevada ties are strongly preferred . Interns will be eligible for a monthly stipend. To apply, please submit the following as one PDF document to NV03Resumes@gmail.com with “Summer 2026 DC Intern - [Last Name], [First Name]” in the subject line: Resume Cover Letter 2 Brief Writing Samples Please submit your application by March 13, 2026 at 11:59 p.m. ET . No phone calls or walk-ins please.

title: Legislative Intern
description_text: Legislative Intern The Office of Congresswoman Sylvia R. Garcia (TX-29) is seeking motivated, detail-oriented, and energetic individuals for a legislative internship in Washington, DC for Summer 2026. This internship will be an invaluable opportunity to dive into the inner workings of Capitol Hill and learn about the legislative process. This is a stipend-paid position. Qualified applicants have strong writing and communication skills, the ability to manage multiple competing tasks, a strong sense of professionalism, and demonstrate a solid work ethic. English and Spanish bilingual candidates with Houston and/or Texas ties are strongly encouraged to apply. Responsibilities include but are not limited to drafting constituent correspondence on a variety of issues, assisting legislative staff with research, leading Capitol tours, answering and screening telephone calls, managing the front office, participating in Committee hearing and markup preparation, drafting memorandums, and other duties as necessary. This position is available beginning May 2026 through August 2026. Interested applicants should send a resume, cover letter, and writing sample to sarah.banafe@mail.house.gov with the subject line “Garcia Summer 2026 Internship Application”. Applications will be accepted until March 13th, 2026, 11:59pm ET . Due to the large volume of applicants, only those applicants who are selected for interviews will be contacted. This office is an equal opportunity employer: Women and minorities are strongly encouraged to apply. This office does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, national origin, disability, military status, age, marital status, or parental status.

title: Digital Manager/Press Assistant
description_text: Rep. Marie Gluesenkamp Perez (WA-03), a distinctive voice in the Democratic party who is also the Administrative Co-Chair of the Blue Dog Coalition, is seeking a Digital Manager/Press Assistant to help amplify the unique interests of her district.  This position reports to the Communications Director and will work across the team to elevate the Member’s communication operation.  Position can be based in the District or DC office. Duties will include, but are not limited to: Producing graphics and videos. Using non-traditional storytelling tools to connect with constituents. Getting creative with platforms like adobe or canva and deploying various social media platforms. Assisting with newsletters, media advisories, speeches, and other press materials. Maintaining the website and overseeing the distribution of daily press clips. Preferred qualifications: Outstanding written and oral communications skills. Ability to manage multiple tasks and think critically in a deadline-driven environment. Commitment to understanding the Member’s unique voice and messaging. Sharp attention to detail. Strong visual intelligence and photography skills. Familiarity with logging, automotive, commercial fishing, or southwest Washington a strong plus. A college degree is not required.  Non-traditional candidates are strongly encouraged to apply. Qualified candidates should submit a cover letter, resume, one writing sample, and one digital sample to WA03jobs@mail.house.gov with the subject line: “Full Name – Digital Manager/Press Assistant."   Please share where you learned of this opportunity in the body of the email. Resumes will be reviewed on a rolling basis until the position is filled.  The office does not discriminate.  Please no phone calls or drop ins.

title: Law Clerk
description_text: The Democratic Staff of the House Committee on Veterans' Affairs seeks a law clerk for the Summer 2026 semester. Responsibilities include, but are not limited to: Conducting legislative and policy research, Drafting memos, letters, and other written materials, Attending briefings and hearings, Assisting with Committee events, and Completing other tasks as assigned. Qualified candidates will be second- or third-year law students who have a demonstrated interest in public service, Congress, or veterans policy. Candidates for this position must have superior organizational skills, a strong work ethic, ability to pay close attention to detail, as well as excellent research, writing, and analytical skills. Additionally, the ability to excel in fast-paced environments and a willingness to assist wherever needed is a must. The Committee strongly encourages veterans, minorities, women, people with disabilities, LGBTQ+ candidates, and HBCU and MSI students to apply. This paid, full-time or part-time, in-person position is based in Washington, D.C. Please complete the online application . The deadline to apply is 5 pm ET on Friday, March 20, 2026 . Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis. Late applications will not be accepted.

title: Legislative Intern
description_text: The Democratic Staff of the House Committee on Veterans' Affairs seeks a legislative intern for the Summer 2026 semester. Responsibilities include, but are not limited to: Conducting research, Managing and responding to constituent correspondence, Assisting with Committee events, Attending briefings and hearings for staff, Drafting memos, Providing administrative support, including answering calls and delivering correspondence, and Other tasks as assigned. A strong work ethic, the ability to excel in fast-paced environments, and a willingness to assist wherever needed is a must. Qualified candidates will have strong oral and written communications skills, be detail-oriented, and have a desire to help veterans and learn about the Committee and the legislative process. The Committee strongly encourages veterans, minority, women, people with disabilities, LGBTQ+ candidates, and HBCU and MSI students to apply. This paid, full-time or part-time, in-person position is based in Washington, D.C. Please complete the online application . The deadline to apply is 5 pm ET on Friday, March 20, 2026 . Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis. Late applications will not be accepted.

title: Press/Digital Intern
description_text: The Democratic Staff of the House Committee on Veterans' Affairs seeks a press/digital intern for the Summer 2026 semester. Responsibilities include, but are not limited to: Compiling and distributing morning press clips, Tracking media hits for Committee members, Assisting with video shoots, Assisting with creating messaging packets and digital toolkits for the caucus, Writing first drafts of remarks, press releases, and video scripts, Developing social media content, Clipping floor and hearing remarks from members, Providing administrative support, including answering calls and delivering correspondence, and Other tasks as assigned. A strong work ethic, the ability to excel in fast-paced environments, and a willingness to assist wherever needed is a must. The ideal candidate will have strong oral and written communications skills, be detail-oriented, have a familiarity with social media platforms, and have a desire to help veterans and learn about the Committee and the legislative process. The Committee strongly encourages veterans, minority, women, people with disabilities, LGBTQ+ candidates, and HBCU and MSI students to apply. This paid, full-time or part-time in-person position is based in Washington, D.C. Please complete the online application . The deadline to apply is 5 pm ET on Friday, March 20, 2026 . Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis. Late applications will not be accepted.

title: Scheduler/Operations Director
description_text: The Office of U.S. Representative Dan Goldman (D-NY-10) is seeking to hire a detail-oriented, reliable, and organized Scheduler OR Operations Director. This position is responsible for managing the Member’s daily and long-term schedule, including organizing and tracking invitations, scheduling requests, and public engagements for the Member; coordinating travel arrangements; and keeping the Member on schedule throughout the day. Additional responsibilities include compiling the Member’s daily briefing materials, as well as other administrative duties, as assigned. Candidates should demonstrate the ability to prioritize multiple projects at once, follow through on requests, have strong attention to detail, and communicate well with team members and external stakeholders in a fast-paced environment. ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS: Prepares the daily schedule and daily memos for the Congressman. Drafts monthly projected schedules for the Congressman. Briefs the Congressman on all scheduling activities and requests of the Washington, D.C. and district offices. Coordinates scheduling of press, interviews, radio and television time with the communications team. Makes reservations for the Congressman's air travel, ground transportation, and lodging. Submits all official travel expenses to the office's finance administrator. Responds to all invitations, either by written correspondence or by personal telephone call. Maintains files of invitations that have been declined, accepted, or that are tentative/pending. EDUCATION/EXPERIENCE: Experience working in a fast-paced, detail oriented environment. Experience organizing a schedule or serving as an executive assistant is preferred. At least one year working in a congressional office is a plus. 2-3 years of professional working experience is preferred. SKILLS AND KNOWLEDGE REQUIRED: Exceptional attention to detail, organizational skills and judgment. Strong oral and written communication skills. Ability to exercise discretion and independent judgment with respect to prioritization of and recommendations on appointments. Ability to work cooperatively and courteously with others. This is not an entry-level position. The annual salary range for this position is $60,000-$80,000 dependent on experience and qualifications. The position offers health coverage; telework opportunities; retirement benefits with an employer match; paid sick, bereavement, and vacation leave; transit or parking benefits; paid parental leave; paid FMLA leave; access to life insurance coverage; professional development opportunities; and access to the Student Loan Repayment Program. Please note that candidates must be available for occasional weekend and evening work to support the Member when the congressional schedule requires. The office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, national origin, age, disability, military status, marital or parental status. Women, people of color, and other members of traditionally underrepresented communities are strongly encouraged to apply. To Apply: Interested candidates are encouraged to submit a cover letter and resume in one PDF document to scheduling.goldman@mail.house.gov with the following subject line: “Team Goldman Scheduler - [candidate’s full name]”.

title: Short-Term Internship
description_text: The Office of Congressman George Whitesides (CA-27) is seeking motivated candidates for a short-term internship in the Washington, D.C. office. This internship offers a unique opportunity to observe the inner workings of Capitol Hill and gain insight into the legislative process. Interns will have the opportunity to work with the legislative, communications, and operations teams. Responsibilities include but are not limited to answering constituent phone calls, giving tours of the Capitol, attending policy briefings and meetings, researching legislation for the legislative staff, and other duties as necessary. The internship will run from the beginning of April to the end of May. Qualified applicants are friendly, detail-oriented, professional, and possess excellent oral and written communication skills. Ties to CA-27 are a plus. Interested applicants should fill out the form linked here and send a cover letter, resume, and a short (one-page) writing sample. Applications will be accepted on a rolling basis. Specify in the cover letter that you are interested in the short-term internship. Please NO calls or walk-ins.

title: Intern
description_text: Summer 2026 Internship The Office of Congressman Frank Pallone Jr. (NJ-06) is seeking qualified candidates for our Summer 2026 internship program in the Washington D.C. office. We are looking for candidates with excitement, an eagerness to learn, customer service skills and strong writing skills. Start and end dates are flexible. New Jersey ties are encouraged but not required. Duties include, but are not limited to: ·         Answering Phone Calls ·         Receiving and logging constituent correspondence on policy matters for the ·         Greeting office visitors ·         Assisting with tour requests and leading tours of the Capitol ·          Drafting constituent correspondence ·         Sorting and distributing mail ·         Attending briefings and hearings, conducting legislative research, and drafting memos ·         Assisting Member and key staff (e.g. Staff Assistant, Scheduler, Chief of Staff) with logistical and administrative duties The summer internship runs from Late May/Early June through Late July/August, with flexible start and end dates. Applicants should submit their application materials here: Internships | Congressman Frank Pallone. Please indicate if you have expertise in digital media, including but not limited to video editing, graphics, and photography. Please NO calls or walk-ins. We are reviewing applications on a rolling basis with a deadline of Friday, March 13 at 11:59 PM EST. All applicants will be considered without regard to race, color, national origin, religion, sex, gender identity, disability, or age. Please indicate if you have expertise in digital media, including but not limited to video editing, graphics, and photography.

title: Press Secretary
description_text: The office of Congressman Jerrold Nadler (NY-12) seeks a motivated and hardworking communications professional to manage communications with both a Washington and a New York focus.  Responsibilities include drafting communications materials (press releases, newsletters, op-eds, written remarks, etc.); maintaining active social media accounts; working closely with both national and local press; updating media lists; and coordinating with legislative staff and district staff.  Qualified candidates must have excellent written and oral communication skills; the ability to comprehend and summarize complex legislation quickly; a proven ability to perform under pressure while juggling multiple tasks; and sound political judgment.  Candidates should have at least 2 years of relevant experience.  Prior Hill or campaign experience, as well as familiarity with New York City political dynamics is preferred. The office of Congressman Nadler strives to ensure that our staff represents the diversity of the country’s population and strongly encourages women and members of historically disadvantaged minority groups to apply. The office of Congressman Nadler does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including marital or parental status, sexual orientation, and gender identity), disability, age, or national origin. Salary range for this position is $60,000 to $75,000 a year. Qualified candidates should submit a cover letter, resume and two writing samples to NY12.Job@mail.house.gov .

title: Communications Director
description_text: United States Representative Jimmy Panetta (CA-19) seeks an experienced and creative Communications Director to lead a dynamic communications operation in a fast-paced, proactive, and collaborative office. The Communications Director will be responsible for developing and executing a comprehensive communications strategy that advances the Representative’s legislative priorities, highlights constituent services, and strengthens connections with local and national media. Responsibilities include: Serving as chief spokesperson and primary point of contact for national, regional, and local press. Drafting and editing press releases, op-eds, newsletters, talking points, speeches, and other written materials. Managing the Representative’s digital presence, including social media platforms, website, and multimedia content. Producing and overseeing video, graphics, and other creative content to expand the office’s reach. Pitching the Representative for media opportunities, op-eds, interviews, and speaking engagements. Coordinating interviews, press conferences, and public events in Washington, D.C. and in California’s 19th Congressional District. Overseeing and managing a robust franking operation, including newsletters, e-blasts, and other constituent communications in compliance with House rules. Monitoring press coverage, providing media analysis, and advising the Representative and staff on messaging. Collaborating with legislative and district staff to ensure consistent, effective communication of policy initiatives and constituent services. Supervising press staff, interns, and district communications support. Qualified applicants will have: Strong writing and editing skills with the ability to translate complex policy into clear, compelling messages. Experience working with reporters and producers; on-the-record experience strongly preferred. Experience with video production, graphic design, or digital storytelling. Familiarity with California’s Central Coast, Spanish-language fluency, or multimedia skills are a plus. Ability to work in a fast-paced environment, manage multiple projects, and respond quickly to breaking news. Commitment to public service, teamwork, and advancing the Representative’s priorities on behalf of the people of California’s 19th Congressional District. Salary & Benefits: Compensation is commensurate with experience, in line with House salary ranges. The office provides a robust benefits package. To Apply: Please send a cover letter, resume, a digital portfolio, two writing samples, and three references to ca19.jobs@mail.house.gov with “Communications Director” in the subject line.  Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis.

title: Staff Assistant
description_text: Congressman Gabe Amo (RI-01) seeks an organized and motivated Staff Assistant to join his office in Washington, D.C. The Staff Assistant will manage the front office, including answering phones and greeting visitors, managing constituent tours and flag requests, coordinating the D.C. internship program, assisting with administrative duties, handling his personal correspondence, driving the Congressman, and other duties as assigned. The Staff Assistant will collaborate closely with the Chief of Staff, Legislative Team, and other staff as appropriate. This is an entry-level position. Ideal candidates will have excellent organizational skills, time management, and communication skills, thrive in a fast-paced, collaborative, and creative environment, and pay close attention to detail. The individual in this role frequently interacts with constituents and visitors on the phone and in person and should have a friendly and professional demeanor. Possession of a driver’s license, a clean driving record, and a vehicle are preferred. Candidates of diverse backgrounds are encouraged to apply. Rhode Island ties are a plus. The office does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including marital or parental status, sexual orientation, and gender identity), disability, age, or national origin. To apply, send in a single PDF with a resume, cover letter, and brief writing sample to RI01AmoJobs@gmail.com , with the following subject line: “Full Name – Staff Assistant.”

title: Legislative Director
description_text: Congresswoman Lateefah Simon, a member of the House Oversight and Small Business committees, seeks a Legislative Director to lead the office’s legislative operation and oversee the development and execution of her policy agenda. The Legislative Director plays an essential leadership role in the office by working closely with the Chief of Staff in assisting with the formulation of the Member’s policy and legislative strategy. The Legislative Director will also work with the Communications Director to ensure that the Member’s legislative strategy is incorporated into the office’s communications plans. The Legislative Director is also responsible for overseeing the legislative team, which currently consists of two legislative assistants and one legislative correspondent.  The LD will be responsible for overseeing the office’s appropriations process, and engaging in community outreach to local stakeholders along with the District Director on the Congresswoman’s agenda. This position will also work closely with the District Director and Chief of Staff to ensure community project funding priorities are included and reflected throughout the appropriations process. The LD will also maintain a policy portfolio and is the lead staffer responsible for identifying and advancing legislative opportunities for the Congresswoman, including drafting legislation, amendments and opportunities to speak on the House Floor on the Congresswoman’s priorities. This position requires long hours, weekends, travel to the district, along with strong leadership and deep knowledge of the legislative process. This is not an entry level position, with a minimum of at least 6 years’ experience a legislative staffer. Strong management skills are required as well. East Bay/California ties are a plus. The Office of Congresswoman Simon is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in employment on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy and gender identity), national origin, political affiliation, sexual orientation, marital status, disability, genetic information, age, membership in an employee organization, retaliation, parental status, military service, or other non-merit factor. Diverse candidates are encouraged to apply. Salary will be based on experience. To apply, please complete this form .  Applications will be considered on a rolling basis. The deadline to apply is Friday, March 13, 2026.

title: Legislative Intern
description_text: The Democratic Staff of the House Energy and Commerce Committee seeks qualified undergrad students and recent graduates for Legislative Interns for the Summer 2026 session, with one internship position available for immediate start . An internship with the Energy and Commerce Democrats is designed to give students and recent graduates a thorough understanding of a congressional committee’s role in the legislative process.  Throughout the course of the program, interns will assist staff with administrative tasks such as answering phones, conducting legislative research, and preparing materials for staff and member briefings.  In addition, interns will have the opportunity to shadow staff at hearings and markups, as well as attend briefings on subjects within the Committee’s broad jurisdiction. Our ideal candidates are organized and committed students or recent graduates eager to join a fast-paced environment and build upon their knowledge of how Congress works—in particular, the role that congressional committees play in the federal legislative process.  Superior writing and communication skills, attention to detail, and a sense of humor are required.  Full-time availability is preferred, but part-time candidates are eligible as well. All applicants will be considered without regard to race, color, national origin, religion, sex (including marital or parental status), sexual orientation, gender identity, disability, or age.  Women, minority, and veteran candidates are highly encouraged to apply.  Interns will receive a stipend, and applicants may request the Committee’s assistance to receive credit from their educational institution. Interested applicants should submit their resume, cover letter, and one writing sample (two pages max.) to InternshipResume.EC@mail.house.gov .  In your cover letter, please indicate preferred start/end dates for scheduling purposes and whether you are available to work full-time or part-time.  Please put “[Immediate Start] Summer 2026 Legislative Intern Application” or “Summer 2026 Legislative Intern Application” in the subject line depending on availability.  Immediate start applications will be considered on a rolling basis with a deadline for applications by Sunday, March 1, 2026.  The deadline for Summer 2026 applications is March 27, 2026. Please no phone calls or drop-ins.

title: Spring Internship
description_text: The Office of Congressman Christian Menefee (TX-18) is excited to invite applications for a Spring Internship in our Washington, D.C. office . This is a unique chance to contribute to meaningful work in a high-energy, dynamic environment, and to make a real impact on the Congressman’s legislative and communications efforts. Responsibilities will include: Helping draft press releases, talking points, statements, and correspondence to constituents Supporting social media, digital communications, and earned media outreach Assisting with research on key policy issues and current events Monitoring media coverage and assisting with rapid response communications Providing logistical support to senior staff across the office We’re looking for someone with strong writing and organizational skills, an interest in public service, and the ability to manage multiple tasks in a fast-paced environment. While prior experience in government or political communications is helpful, it’s not required. What matters most is your passion, your commitment, and your desire to learn and grow. This internship provides an invaluable opportunity to gain firsthand experience in Congress and be part of a team that is working to move the country forward. If you're ready to take on a challenge and make a difference, we’d love to hear from you. Please submit your resume and cover letter to apply here . Texas ties are a plus.

title: Legislative Director
description_text: Legislative Director — Office of Congressman George Whitesides (CA-27) The Office of Congressman George Whitesides is building a team dedicated to solving the nation’s most important problems. We are seeking a Legislative Director to lead his legislative operation and drive a fast-paced agenda across policy areas like Abundance (housing, energy, healthcare, jobs, safety), Science & Innovation, Structural Reform, Teen Online Safety, and Nature/Climate. Rep. Whitesides serves on the House Armed Services Committee (HASC) and the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology (SST). Core Responsibilities Lead and execute the Member’s legislative strategy; coalition build around legislative  priorities, provide vote recommendations and policy counsel in close coordination with the Chief of Staff. Manage, mentor, and develop legislative staff; set priorities, timelines, and quality control across deliverables. Drive committee work and stakeholder engagement tied to the Congressman’s work on HASC and SST, including drafting/advancing legislation, oversight, and coalition-building. Coordinate closely with the District Office and communications team to deliver tangible wins for CA-27 and message them effectively. Requirements Demonstrated leadership, management, and problem-solving experience. Strong knowledge of House procedure and a track record of securing legislative wins. Hill experience preferred (not required for exceptional candidates). This is not an entry-level position. To Apply The office is an equal opportunity employer. Qualified candidates should submit a cover letter and resume via this form by Thursday, February 26th .

title: Summer Internship Program- 2026
description_text: The Office of Congresswoman Norma Torres is currently seeking full-time summer 2026 interns for her DC office. These paid internships are an excellent opportunity for those interested in public service and learning more about the legislative process. Interns will have the opportunity to be hands-on in the legislative process by assisting with Rep. Torres’ responsibilities on the House Administration and Appropriations Committees. Our office seeks to help interns grow beyond the typical intern responsibilities by incorporating their passions and goals into their internship. Additionally, a specific press internship is also available to work directly with the Press Secretary to develop and implement communications and social media strategies. Intern responsibilities include researching legislation, attending hearings and briefings, drafting constituent letters, assisting the press and legislative teams in pushing policy forward, drafting memoranda for the staff, and performing administrative work as needed. To submit your application, please visit https://torres.house.gov/services/internships to complete the form and email CA35.Internship@mail.house.gov a one-page resume, cover letter, and your availability with the subject line as "[Last Name] - Summer 2026 DC Internship Application." Applications will be considered until March 23, 2026. Ties to CA-35 and California, along with Spanish skills, are preferred but not required. The office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate based on race, religion, disability, age, ethnicity, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, family status, or military status.

title: Press/Digital Assistant
description_text: Representative Seth Magaziner (RI-02) seeks a Press/Digital Assistant in his Warwick, RI office for duties including but not limited to: Supporting the comprehensive communications, media, and digital strategy of the Office to promote the Member’s policies and priorities; Working with the Communications Director to identify new ways to engage with press and digital audiences; Assisting the Communications Director in drafting press releases, advisories, talking points, newsletters, social media content, digital graphics, and other materials; Assisting with writing and designing innovative social and digital content, and video editing; Staffing the Member for in-district press events, interviews, and other district events as assigned by the District Director; Monitoring and compiling daily press clips, traditional media coverage, and social media engagement and metrics. Applicants must possess a valid driver’s license and own and maintain a vehicle registered in Rhode Island. Some occasional driving of the Member will be necessary, as assigned. The ideal candidate will possess strong writing skills, experience with video and graphic design, and an interest in traditional and digital media strategy. Relevant digital or social media experience is preferred. The Press Assistant reports to the Communications Director and District Director. Applicants should send, as a single PDF file, a resume, cover letter, and three references with “Press/Digital Assistant” in the subject line to ri02magazinerjobs@mail.house.gov. The PDF file should be titled “Press/Digital Assistant – LastName, FirstName” (e.g. “Press/Digital Assistant – Doe, Jane.pdf ”). Female, minority, and LGBTQ+ candidates are strongly encouraged to apply. The office is an equal opportunity employer; we do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, gender identity or expression, or sexual orientation.

title: Legislative Correspondent/Legislative Aide
description_text: Congresswoman Nellie Pou (NJ-09) is seeking a Legislative Correspondent/Legislative Aide to join her congressional office in Washington, DC. The LC/LA will be responsible for managing the office’s constituent correspondence program. The position will also oversee a significant policy portfolio including labor, energy, and environment, and assist the Legislative Director in handing the Congresswoman’s work on the House Transportation and Infrastructure Committee. Candidates should be experienced on Capitol Hill; this is not an entry level position. Excellent oral and written communication skills, creativity, team collaboration, the ability to thrive under pressure and meet tight deadlines, and a commitment to working non-standard hours, especially when the House is in session, are essential attributes.  This is a writing-intensive position. The ability to speak and write in Spanish is a plus, as is a connection to New Jersey. DUTIES: Managing the office’s constituent correspondence program and drafting letters; Meeting with constituents, advocates, and stakeholders; Drafting talking points, memos, and policy materials; Developing legislative initiatives to help advance the Congresswoman’s policy priorities; Staff the Congresswoman at hearings, meetings, and relevant events; and Other duties as assigned. Please submit a resume and cover letter to nj09job@gmail.com with “LC/LA (First Name Last Name)” in the subject line to apply. Rep. Pou is an equal opportunity employer. Women, veterans, people of color, and LGBTQ+ persons are encouraged to apply.

title: Legislative Intern
description_text: The Democratic Staff of the Committee on Rules is seeking full-time, energetic interns for the summer (June - August). Our interns work closely with Committee staff to prepare materials for Committee meetings, conduct research projects, learn about the Rules of the House and the various procedures for legislation to reach the House Floor, and complete a variety of other administrative tasks. Ideal candidates have strong writing skills, a good work ethic, an interest in learning about House floor procedures, and are detail-oriented. Interns will gain experience in legislative analysis, political communications, and institutional processes. A stipend will be available for qualified candidates. Massachusetts ties are a plus. All offers are conditional on completion of a federal criminal background check. The office is an equal opportunity employer and strongly encourages candidates from diverse backgrounds or with untraditional experience to apply. Interested applicants should submit a resume and cover letter explaining why you want to join the Democratic staff of the House Rules Committee to democrats-rules.house.gov/about/internships . No calls or drop-ins, please. The deadline to apply is March 15, 2026. Note: Students on quarter systems or with unusual schedules are still encouraged to apply—please detail your particular situation in your cover letter.

title: Digital Director or Digital Assistant
description_text: The Office of Rep. Becca Balint (VT-AL) seeks a creative and motivated Digital Director or Digital Assistant ( title commensurate with experience) to support the office’s fast-paced and high-visibility digital communications and outreach operation. The ideal candidate has experience creating high quality graphics, video, and overall digital strategy to communicate with clarity and creativity. This role will be based in our Washington, DC office. The Digital Director/Assistant will be responsible for managing the Congresswoman’s social media channels and creating a digital strategy for platform growth, under the supervision of the Communications Director. The ideal candidate has: Strong communications skills (writing, editing, proofreading) Creative thinking and a willingness to take risks and try new things Clear sense of successful social media writing; narrative voice Excellent ability to perform rapid response based on breaking news and social media trends Understanding of online social media tools including platforms like Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, Threads, Twitter, Adobe Premier, etc. Experience producing excellent written and visual content, including graphics and video, for a variety of social media platforms Ability to develop effective and long-term digital growth strategy Demonstrated ability to lead/contribute to social media planning for a progressive voice Thorough knowledge of the news cycle and ability to closely follow issues Congresswoman is focused on A strong team ethos and desire to be a part of a learning culture Responsibilities include but are not limited to: Supporting implementation of digital communications by developing and executing digital strategy aimed at platform growth Drafting social media content plans, writing social media copy, assisting with press materials, creating graphics and videos (includes clipping and captioning) Managing the Congresswoman’s website and leading monthly e-newsletters Assisting Communications Director with creating coherent messaging across social media and traditional press appearances Tracking social media analytics Supporting the Outreach team and Communications Director with logistics for town halls, press conferences, and other public events, both virtual and in-person Previous Hill or campaign experience preferred. Vermont ties a plus. This office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, gender identity, or sexual orientation. Please send a resume, cover letter, 2-3 digital samples and 2-3 references to balintresumes118@gmail.com with the subject line “First name Last name – Digital.” The starting salary for this position is $65,000 for a Digital Director or $55,000 for a Digital Assistant. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis with a final deadline of February 26, 2026.

title: Communications Director
description_text: The Office of Congresswoman Maggie Goodlander (NH-02) is seeking a Communications Director (title commensurate with experience) in her Washington, D.C. office. This role leads the Congresswoman’s communications strategy and works closely with senior staff to ensure strong coordination across legislative, communications, and district teams. For the right candidate, the role may include additional senior management responsibilities. Core responsibilities include developing and executing a proactive, integrated communications strategy; leading earned media, digital, and social media efforts; overseeing press releases, statements, op-eds, speeches, talking points, and constituent communications; managing rapid response and crisis communications; cultivating relationships with national, state, and local media; supervising communications staff; and coordinating with external consultants. The Communications Director will work closely with the Chief of Staff, Legislative Director, and district team to advance the Congresswoman’s priorities for New Hampshire. The ideal candidate will have strong political judgment, excellent organizational and writing skills, and the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment. Experience in political communications, government, or advocacy is required; management experience and familiarity with New Hampshire’s media and political landscape are preferred but not required. Salary starts at $100,000 and is commensurate with experience. New Hampshire ties are a plus. APPLY HERE.

title: Legislative Assistant
description_text: The Office of Congressman Mike Quigley (IL-05) seeks a Legislative Assistant to manage a broad portfolio of issues, including transportation, trade, small business, tax, financial services, permitting, and telecommunications policy. This position will have primary responsibility for transportation policy related to Congressman Quigley’s role as a senior member of the Transportation, Housing, and Urban Development Appropriations Subcommittee. This is a full-time, in-person position in the Washington, DC office. A strong candidate will have Chicago ties, including experience in Chicago related to transportation and/or economic policy. The ideal candidate will be able to maintain strong relationships with stakeholders, manage both short- and long-term projects, bring bold policy ideas, think creatively to achieve policy objectives, and be excited to work for an active Member and team. Any member of Congressman Quigley’s team should possess the following characteristics: Willingness to work flexible hours; this is not always a 9-to-5 position. Growth mindset, openness to feedback, and desire to grow with the team. Hustle, grit, and perseverance. Ability to think critically and make sound decisions. Creativity and solution-oriented approach to delivering results. Excellent oral and written communication skills and strong organizational abilities. Proven relationship-management and project-management experience. Great sense of humor, ability to laugh at dad jokes. This is not an entry-level position. Salary range is $70k - $80k, commensurate with experience. Candidates from diverse backgrounds and underrepresented communities are strongly encouraged to apply.  The U.S. House of Representatives is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, military status, gender identity and expression, marital status, or sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. The deadline for applications is Monday, February 23, at 5pm . To apply, qualified candidates should submit a cover letter (.pdf), resume (.pdf), and writing sample (.pdf) to Charlie Chamness ( charles.chamness@mail.house.gov ), Cc Liza Lynch ( liza.lynch@mail.house.gov ) with “Legislative Assistant — (Name)” in the subject line. No phone calls or drop-ins, please.

title: Legislative Director
description_text: Moderate House Democrat seeks qualified candidates for the position of Legislative Director for their Washington, D.C. office. Responsibilities include: Leading the development and implementation of the Member’s short-term and long-term legislative priorities with the Member and Chief of Staff. Monitoring and managing activity on the House Floor, and working with the legislative team to develop vote recommendations. Managing the legislative team and tracking a high volume of legislative projects to ensure their timely completion. Developing and reviewing memos, remarks, committee materials, and other briefing materials for the Member on relevant topics. Proactively monitor news and developments related to the Member’s policy priorities in order to keep them informed and proactively propose additional actions. Working closely with the Chief of Staff and the communications team on effectively messaging legislative initiatives and preparing the Member for media appearances. Managing a portfolio of high level legislative projects, including assisting and advising the Member in their work in high profile negotiations. Ideal candidates will have: A Bachelor’s degree or related job experience. 3-5 years of legislative experience in a Congressional office or legislative environment Knowledge of the legislative process and procedures. Excellent attention to detail and exceptionally strong organizational skills. Sound judgment and the ability to anticipate and apply the Member’s perspective when working on policy issues. Strong communication skills. Capacity to communicate with a wide variety of personalities in a tactful, courteous, and professional manner. Willingness to accept direction and guidance on performance and process improvements from the Member and the Chief of Staff. The ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment. The ability to work flexible hours including long hours, nights, and weekends. The ability to anticipate challenges and proactively offer solutions. This position reports to the Chief of Staff. Notice: This office is an equal opportunity employer; we do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, disability, military status, parental status, marital status, sexual orientation, or any other factor or basis prohibited by applicable federal law.  Applicants with disabilities who require reasonable accommodation to participate in the application or hiring processes are encouraged to request an accommodation at any time during those processes. Applicant Instructions: All interested candidates are encouraged to submit a resume and cover letter with “LD - Last Name” as the subject line to Jobs@mail.house.gov .

title: Intern
description_text: Congresswoman Suzan DelBene, representing the 1st Congressional District of Washington state, seeks interns for her Washington, D.C. and District offices. Interns will work in a fast-paced congressional office, gaining valuable experience and knowledge of the United States Congress. Intern responsibilities include greeting constituents and handling constituent phone calls, assisting staff with administrative tasks, conducting research, and assisting with constituent correspondence. Kirkland, WA interns will have the opportunity to attend community events and tours with the Congresswoman and staff. Washington, D.C. interns will have the opportunity to lead tours of the U.S. Capitol as well as attend hearings and briefings for the legislative staff. Qualifications: The ideal candidates will possess exceptional written and oral communication skills, a high degree of professionalism, as well as a strong interest in public service. Washington state ties are a plus but are not required. Important Information: Congressional internships are open to all majors and designed for undergraduate and graduate students, as well as recent graduates with an interest in public service. Starting and ending dates are flexible, although prospective interns should be able to commit to at least 17 hours per week during the internship. A stipend of $1,000/month will be provided to full-time interns, and a stipend of $500/month will be provided to part time interns. Apply: To apply, please submit a cover letter, resume, and short writing sample (1 to 2 pages) through this form. The deadline for Summer 2026 Internship Applications is 11:59 PM on Sunday, March 13th. Due to a high volume of applications, you may only be contacted if you are selected for an interview. This office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate based on race, religion, disability, age, ethnicity, national origin, sex, gender, LGBTQ identification, family status, or military status. Women, people of color, and other members of traditionally underrepresented communities are strongly encouraged to apply. No phone calls or walk ins - please email internship.delbene@mail.house.gov with any questions or concerns. FAQ What month does this internship start? Winter/Spring internships start in January and go through mid-May, Summer internships start in May/June and go through August, and Fall internships start in September and go through December. Specific start and end dates can be flexible based on school schedules.

title: Digital Intern
description_text: Summer 2026 Digital Intern Digital interns will be responsible for compiling and distributing press clips, helping develop digital content including graphics and videos, and drafting copy for social media and press materials. Digital interns will also have the opportunity to staff the Congresswoman to capture videos and photos of events. Successful interns in our office are adaptable and proactive self-starters who have experience in a fast-paced, deadline-oriented work environment. Applicants must be independent problem-solvers, have strong writing skills, and be comfortable communicating to both internal and external audiences. An ideal digital intern has technical experience with video editing software (Premiere, Headliner) and/or graphic design tools (Canva, InDesign, Illustrator). Spanish language skills are preferred but not required. Prior volunteer, leadership, and work experience, ties to PA-06, and experience with photo editing programs (Photoshop, Lightroom) are all a plus. To apply, please send: 1) a resume, 2) ONE brief paragraph about yourself and why you’re interested in interning in our office, 3) a writing sample, and 4) three examples of digital work (video, graphic or photo) to PA06Press.Internships@mail.house.gov . Deadline to Apply is March 15, 2026.

title: Staff Assistant / Driver
description_text: STAFF ASSISTANT / DRIVER Office of Congresswoman Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick (FL-20) Staff Assistant/Driver – The Office of Congresswoman Cherfilus-McCormick, a member of the House Foreign Affairs Committee (HFAC) and House Veterans’ Affairs (HVAC) seeks a Staff Assistant / Driver to support the Member Office’s operations efforts. The Staff Assistant / Driver greets visitors, answers the phone, drives the Member, and answers constituent requests for general information including tour and flag requests. This position also monitors delivery and pickup of materials, maintains the front office, and assists with various administrative, operations and legislative duties. The staff member in this role interacts regularly with constituents and should have a friendly, helpful demeanor. The ideal candidate is a team player, strong writer and researcher, skilled listener and communicator, detail-oriented, organized, responsive and accessible, a creative problem solver, and self-motivated. The candidate should be capable of managing time effectively and prioritizing assignments with tact. The position requires the individual to be available for extended and unscheduled hours (including mornings/nights, weekends and/or holidays). This is an entry level position. Applicants must possess a valid driver’s license with a clean driving record, and a vehicle. ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS: ·         Manages the front office; ·         Answers and screens telephone calls for the Congresswoman and other staff members and takes messages when appropriate; ·         Greets and screens visitors; ·         Provides staff-led tours of the Capitol, The White House and other identified agencies for constituents and other guests; ·         Assists persons who have meetings with the Congresswoman or the staff; ·         Works closely with the Congresswoman’s Scheduler to ensure that the Member’s appointments are on time and that the Scheduler, and member’s team are aware of the visitor's arrival; ·         Maintains literature regarding the district and House offices for distribution to visitors; ·         Purchases supplies for the office from the House Office Supply store as instructed by the Office Manager; ·         Signs for deliveries and forwards all materials delivered to the office to appropriate staff members in a timely manner; ·         Ensures that requests for assistance are directed to the appropriate staff member in a timely manner; ·         Documents visitor opinions on issues communicated via telephone, including full name, address, and all relevant information as appropriate;
