·         Performs or coordinates administrative assignments with interns; ·         Performs special projects assigned by the Congresswoman or Chief of Staff; ·         Maintains a good working relationship with the Congresswoman, Chief of Staff, department staff, and constituents; ·         Accepts performance-based criticism and direction; ·         Possesses a temperament to communicate with a variety of personalities in a tactful, pleasant, and professional manner; ·         Meets attendance requirements as established by the office; ·         Works well under pressure and handles stress; ·         Works a flexible schedule including long hours, nights, and weekends as needed according to the Congressional schedule; ·         Performs other duties as assigned. EDUCATION/EXPERIENCE: Must possess a bachelor’s degree. Congressional experience, including internships, is preferred. A minimum of 2 years of related experience or training in public administration, project management, or hospitality is preferred. SALARY AND BENEFITS: Starting salary range is $50,000 annually. The position offers health coverage; retirement benefits with an employer match; paid sick, bereavement, and vacation leave; 6-12 weeks of paid FMLA leave; transit or parking benefits; access to life insurance coverage; and access to the Student Loan Repayment Program. The office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate based on race, religion, disability, age, ethnicity, national origin, sex, gender, LGBTQ identification, family status, or military status. Women, people of color, and other members of traditionally underrepresented communities are strongly encouraged to apply. TO APPLY: This is an urgent hire. Applications will be accepted until the position is filled. The office will contact the best-qualified candidates to begin the interview process. To apply, please email FL20jobs@mail.house.gov with “Staff Assistant/Driver” in the subject line and the following materials: (1) cover letter, (2) resume, (3) transcript, (4) two references including name, current title and organization, phone number and email.

title: Legislative Intern
description_text: Our office offers part- and full-time internships for students who are in college or a similar post-high school training program, or who have just graduated. We are dedicated to fostering the continued success of our interns beyond their time with our office. Former interns of ours have gone on to work in other offices across Capitol Hill, on state-wide political campaigns, and in various profit and non-profit organizations. Internships generally run in the Spring (Jan-May), Summer (May-Aug), and Fall (Sep-Dec). We offer stipends to students, with accommodations for those who are Pell Grant eligible. Our internship program has returned to in-person work, and we are seeking interns for our Reading, West Chester, and Washington, D.C. offices. Ideal applicants are self-starters who bring a customer service mindset and a passion for community service, who are intellectually curious, who don’t mind spending a lot of time on the phone, and who write well. Responsibilities range from helping constituents, doing research and drafting memos for our legislative staff, assisting our communications team, to staffing the Congresswoman in hearings or meetings. We offer interns the opportunity to see Congress up close and from the inside. Prior volunteer, leadership, and work experience are a plus. Ties to PA-06 are also a plus. Due to a high volume of applicants, you will likely only be contacted if you are selected for an interview. This office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate based on race, religion, disability, age, ethnicity, national origin, sex, gender, LGBTQ identification, family status, or military status. Women, people of color, and other members of traditionally underrepresented communities are strongly encouraged to apply. If you have any questions, please reach out to Nirmal Singletary at nirmal.singletary@mail.house.gov.

title: Legislative Intern
description_text: Congresswoman Kelly Morrison (MN-03) is accepting applications for paid full-time, and part-time internships in her Washington, D.C. office for the Summer 2026 semester. The ideal candidate will have strong written and oral communication skills, an interest in the legislative process, and a desire to learn in a fast-paced environment. Applicants should be organized, dependable, and eager to contribute to a collaborative team. Ties to Minnesota or the Upper Midwest are preferred but not required. Responsibilities include answering phones, drafting constituent correspondence, conducting legislative research, attending hearings and briefings, leading Capitol tours, and assisting staff with additional projects as needed. The internship will begin in Late May/early June 2026 and run through August 2026, with flexible start and end dates. Applicants should be available at least 20 hours per week. This position is in-person. The application deadline is April 3, 2026, though applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis, and early submission is encouraged. Due to the volume of applications, only those selected for interviews will be contacted. To apply, please submit a resume and cover letter  as a single PDF titled “LastnameFirstname.pdf” at MN03Internships@mail.house.gov with the subject line "MN03 Summer 2026 (DC) Internship” Congresswoman Morrison’s office is an equal opportunity employer. Women, people of color, LGBTQ+ individuals, and those from diverse backgrounds are strongly encouraged to apply. The office does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, disability, military status, age, marital status, or parental status.

title: DC Communications Director
description_text: Congresswoman Melanie Stansbury (NM-01) is hiring a strategic DC Communications Director to support her work on behalf of New Mexico’s First Congressional District. This is not an entry level position. Previous experience on Capitol Hill and/or high-level political communications experience is required, along with a solid understanding of national television booking, Congress, and the legislative process. The DC Communications Director will be joining a dedicated, hard-working, and upbeat team defending our democracy and serving the people of New Mexico. This role will work alongside our New Mexico Communications Director, report to the Chief of Staff and Member, and co-manage a communications assistant who is based in DC. Job responsibilities will include strategic planning, drafting speeches, written remarks, press statements, some digital work, and more: The ideal candidate will also possess the following: ·         Hill and national media contacts. ·         Ability to book, prepare, and staff national media appearances. ·         Exceptional writing and researching skills. ·         Ability to work long hours (including weekends and evenings as needed), under pressure, while remaining collaborative and effective. ·         Thoroughness and attention to detail. ·         Discretion and sound judgment. ·         An independent self-starter and team-player. ·         Strong organizational, time-management, and project management skills with the ability to juggle multiple priorities simultaneously. ·         Self-starting, high dependability, adaptability, and organization skills. ·         Manage and mentor junior staff. Strong preference for New Mexico ties, where possible. This office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, sex (including marital or parental status), color, age, religion, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, or disability. Interested candidates must submit a resume to repstansburyresumes@mail.house.gov by March 7 with the following subject line: “Last Name – DC Communications Director.” Interviews will be conducted on a rolling basis. Out of fairness to all candidates, those who do not submit their materials to this email will not be considered. Qualified candidates should submit a single PDF saved with LastName.FirstName.DCCommunicationsDirector to include: 1. Cover letter 2. Resume 3. One writing sample 4. One video media sample 5. One graphic media sample

title: Legislative Correpsondent
description_text: The Office of Congresswoman Maggie Goodlander (NH-02) is seeking a Legislative Correspondent in her Washington, D.C. office to manage the office’s constituent correspondence program and support the legislative team. This position requires strong writing skills, attention to detail, and the ability to manage high volumes of time-sensitive work. Core responsibilities include drafting timely, accurate, and substantive responses to constituent mail and email; maintaining and updating the office’s letter library; tracking and analyzing constituent input for senior staff; conducting legislative and policy research; and assisting with memos, talking points, and background materials. The Legislative Correspondent will work closely with the Legislative Director and legislative team to ensure constituent feedback informs policy priorities and office decision-making. The ideal candidate will have exceptional writing and editing skills, strong organizational abilities, and sound judgment. Familiarity with the legislative process and constituent correspondence systems is preferred, as is the ability to work collaboratively in a fast-paced environment. New Hampshire ties are a plus.

title: Communications Director
description_text: The Office of Congresswoman Maggie Goodlander (NH-02) is seeking a Communications Director (title commensurate with experience) in her Washington, D.C. office. This role leads the Congresswoman’s communications strategy and works closely with senior staff to ensure strong coordination across legislative, communications, and district teams. For the right candidate, the role may include additional senior management responsibilities. Core responsibilities include developing and executing a proactive, integrated communications strategy; leading earned media, digital, and social media efforts; overseeing press releases, statements, op-eds, speeches, talking points, and constituent communications; managing rapid response and crisis communications; cultivating relationships with national, state, and local media; supervising communications staff; and coordinating with external consultants. The Communications Director will work closely with the Chief of Staff, Legislative Director, and district team to advance the Congresswoman’s priorities for New Hampshire. The ideal candidate will have strong political judgment, excellent organizational and writing skills, and the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment. Experience in political communications, government, or advocacy is required; management experience and familiarity with New Hampshire’s media and political landscape are preferred but not required. Salary starts at $100,000 and is commensurate with experience. New Hampshire ties are a plus.

title: Summer 2026 Internship
description_text: The Washington, D.C. office of Congresswoman Gwen Moore (WI-04) is seeking interns for the upcoming Summer 2026 term. Responsibilities include answering phones, attending hearings and briefings, drafting and editing constituent correspondence letters, writing memos, conducting research for the legislative staff, and otherwise providing support for the day-to-day activities of the office. Interns are paid via a monthly stipend. The Office of Congresswoman Moore is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based on race, ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, religion, national origin, age, or socioeconomic status. All are welcome and encouraged to apply. Interested applicants should send a resume, cover letter, and two short writing samples to WI04DC.Internships@mail.house.gov . Additionally, applicants should indicate their availability on an hourly, daily, and monthly basis during the Summer term. The deadline to apply is 6:00pm ET on Friday, April 17th, 2026.

title: Staff Assistant/Legislative Correspondent
description_text: Congressman David Scott (GA-13), senior member of the House Financial Services Committee and House Agriculture Committee, seeks a Staff Assistant/Legislative Correspondent to join his Washington, D.C. office. Core responsibilities include: •             Handling incoming calls and capturing constituent requests •             Executing the Member’s constituent correspondence program by drafting/editing letters and ensuring all constituents receive responses in a timely manner •             Consults with the Legislative Director and Legislative Assistants to ensure the Member's library of legislative letters are accurate, up to date, and reflective of the Member's policy position •             Working closely with the Member’s Director of Operations to ensure timely arrival of all appointments, including by driving the Member •             Managing the front office, including by signing for deliveries, performing administrative duties, greeting constituents and visitors to provide a welcoming environment, and more •             Assists with constituent flag requests, tours of the Capitol, and arranging visits to other national landmarks to assist with constituent relations •             Overseeing and managing the intern program, including their schedule and training •             Providing relevant information to constituents to fulfill their requests •             Purchasing supplies for the office from the House Office Supply store to maintain office equipment •             Other tasks as assigned Qualifications: •             Applicants must own a car as they will drive the Member •             Bachelor’s degree or equivalent education and experience preferred •             Prior constituent services and management experience preferred •             Ties to GA-13 or Georgia preferred, but not required Interested candidates should e-mail a cover letter, resumé, and one writing sample to Georgia13Resumes@gmail.com with the subject line, “Last Name, First Name, SA/LC”. The deadline for applying is Wednesday, February 18th, COB. Salary range is commensurate with experience and includes health insurance, student loan repayment, and other benefits. The Office of Congressman David Scott is an Equal Opportunity Employer and does not discriminate in employment on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy and gender identity), national origin, political affiliation, sexual orientation, marital status, disability, genetic information, age, membership in an employee organization, retaliation, parental status, or military service. Diverse candidates are encouraged to apply.

title: Digital Director / Press Secretary
description_text: DIGITAL DIRECTOR / PRESS SECRETARY – The office of Congressman Josh Riley is seeking a creative and adaptable Digital Director / Press Secretary to join a fast-paced communications team based out of the Washington, D.C. office. This role requires a versatile communicator capable of balancing press writing with digital production. The ideal candidate will possess 1 to 2 years of experience in digital media and political communications, with a proven ability to manage daily digital operations and translate complex legislation into compelling, accessible content. Ties to Upstate New York are a plus. Responsibilities: Direct the office’s social media strategy, managing a comprehensive content calendar from concept to execution. Write and edit press materials, including releases, advisories, talking points, and the constituent newsletter. Capture high quality photos and video of the Congressman at events and in meetings. Staff and support logistics for the Congressman's interviews, press conferences, and other public-facing events, both virtual and in-person. Coordinate joint press efforts and shared messaging with other Member and Committee offices. Compile daily news clips and monitor regional trends for rapid-response opportunities. The salary range for this position is $70,000-$80,000, commensurate with experience. This position will sometimes require evening and weekend work and travel to the district. The Digital Director / Press Secretary will report to the Communications Director based in New York. To apply, please email rileyny19jobs@gmail.com with “Digital Director / Press Secretary” in the subject line and the following application materials attached a single combined PDF: Resume Two professional writing samples Portfolio of social media content, including graphic and video design work The office is an equal opportunity employer; we do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, national origin, disability, military status, age, marital status, or parental status. Candidates of diverse backgrounds are encouraged to apply.

title: Legislative Director
description_text: LEGISLATIVE DIRECTOR SUMMARY: Congresswoman Jahana Hayes (CT-05), a Member of the Education and Workforce Committee and House Agriculture Committee seeks an experienced Legislative Director to oversee her legislative team and policy agenda. The Legislative Director is a senior member of the leadership team in our office, who works extremely closely with the Member and senior staff. The Legislative Director will serve as the primary policy advisor – providing strategic direction and insight to the Member and team on all legislative and policy matters, while being responsible for overseeing the Member’s legislative and policy agenda, overseeing the legislative team, and collaborating closely with senior leadership to solve complex problems and deliver results. Additionally, this person will provide direct supervision over the Legislative team. ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS •	Ensures that the legislative staff is properly focused on the Congresswoman's legislative goals; •	Works to formulate positions on legislative issues; •	Write recommendations for each vote the Congresswoman takes; •	Monitors legislative activity on the House floor and handles long-term and short-term legislative planning for the Congresswoman; •	Oversees the progression of bills with which the Congresswoman is involved as they move from Committee to the floor; •	Assigns legislative staff areas of responsibility, in consultation with the Congresswoman and Chief of Staff; •	Trains and supervises legislative staff; •	Schedules and directs legislative staff meetings; •	Meets with groups of key constituents or other office visitors, as necessary; •	Recommends co-sponsorships of legislation to the Congresswoman; •	Writes and reviews legislative memos; •	Hires legislative staff, with approval of the Chief of Staff and the Congresswoman, when necessary; •	Oversees activities of the office in the absence of the Chief of Staff; •	Maintains up-to-date status reports on all legislation affecting the district, legislation in which the Congresswoman is a principal sponsor, or legislation which the Congresswoman is a cosponsor; •	Monitors and reports on floor action to the Congresswoman and the Chief of Staff; •	Prepares weekly reports for the Congresswoman and staff on all legislative activities; •	Coordinates responses to all legislative mail; •	Assists organizations and constituents in the district with federal matters; and •	Performs other duties as assigned. QUALIFICATIONS •	This is not an entry-level position. At least 4 years of relevant legislative experience is required. Applicants who do not meet these requirements will not be considered; •	Hard work, organization, effective time management, and ability to work in a fast-paced and collaborative environment; •	Analytical capabilities, excellent writing skills, attention to detail, flexibility, and creativity; •	Willingness to cover a diverse issue portfolio and be responsive to varied concerns and perspectives; •	Expert knowledge of the legislative process; •	Strong oral and written communications skills and ability to communicate effectively with the Congresswoman, colleagues, constituents, and external stakeholders. We are an equal opportunity employer. Interested parties should e-mail a concise cover letter including salary requirements, a resume, and two writing samples to CT05jobs@mail.house.gov with the following subject line: Full Name – Legislative Director. No phone calls or walk-ins, please.

title: Field Representative
description_text: The Office of Congresswoman Gluesenkamp Perez (WA-03) is seeking a Field Representative to be the primary person handling outreach and issues in the trades community in Southwest Washington.  This position serves as a member of the Congresswoman District Office with the option to be based in the Congresswoman’s Vancouver or Kelso office. This role will work closely with the Congresswoman and her team to meet with local stakeholders, businesses, and organizations—with an emphasis on focusing on trades and technical stakeholders. Background: Congresswoman Gluesenkamp Perez co-owns an auto repair and machine shop with her husband and has made the trades and technical pathways a focus during her time in Congress. In D.C., she has advocated for honors-level shop class, funding for local CTE programs, and ensuring young people can use 529 plans to pay for tools instead of 4-year college. Back home, she’s visited CTE programs in all seven counties in her district and has advocated for state-level changes to allow 6th graders in CTE programs. The Field Representative will help the Congresswoman engage with constituents in the trades to help inform this important work. Essential duties: Acts as a liaison between the Congresswoman, District Director, and other senior staff and stakeholders. Informs the Congresswoman and team of updates to his or her assigned issue area by screening district media and interacting with constituents. Builds durable relationships with stakeholders and provides updates to them on the Congresswoman’s work. Briefs and staffs the Congresswoman at events in the district. Travels throughout the district to meet with constituents and stakeholder groups on issues related to the Congresswoman’s priorities. Acts as a representative of the office at events, including updating groups on the Congresswoman’s work and speaking on her behalf. Collaborates with the District Director and DC legislative staff to ensure district priorities inform the Congresswoman’s legislative agenda. Preferred qualifications: A college degree is not required.  Non-traditional candidates are strongly encouraged to apply. Ties to Southwest Washington strongly encouraged. Individuals with a background as a tradesperson, craftsman, or skilled worker are highly encouraged to apply. Strong interpersonal and relationship-building skills, with a commitment to public service and constituent engagement. Ability to manage multiple priorities, work independently, and collaborate with a team. This full time position is expected to work non-traditional hours and to regularly travel throughout Southwest Washington.  Salary for this position is $50,000-60,000, commensurate with experience. The office does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including marital or parental status, sexual orientation, and gender identity), disability, age, or national origin. Qualified candidates should submit a cover letter, resume, and a paragraph each about two recent projects they’ve worked on to WA03jobs@mail.house.gov with the subject line: “Full Name – Field Representative." Photos of recent projects are encouraged.  Please share where you learned of this opportunity in the body of the email. Resumes will be reviewed on a rolling basis until the position is filled.  Please no phone calls or drop ins.

title: Digital Manager; Press Assistant
description_text: Position Overview: The Digital/Press Manager will work closely with and report to the Communications Director and play a pivotal role in supporting the creation and execution of the Congressman’s digital and media strategy. This position will focus on maintaining an active online presence, creating digital content, managing social media platforms, and drafting written products to support the Congressman’s goals and priorities. The ideal candidate will be a self-starter and possess strong writing skills, keen attention to detail, and be able to work independently in a fast-paced environment. This role is based in the DC office. The salary for this role is $55,000, commensurate with experience. Key Responsibilities: Design creative digital content including graphics and videos to communicate key messages and legislative priorities; Staff, photograph, and film the Congressman at scheduled events and meetings; Create compelling social media content that uplifts the Congressman’s priorities; Maintain and update the Congressman’s website, ensuring timely and relevant information is publicly available; Provide first drafts of press releases, speeches, talking points, and other written materials; Draft and send the weekly email newsletter to constituents, keeping them informed about Congressman’s work and priorities; Collaborating with the Legislative and District teams to proactively identify and execute opportunities that elevate Congressman’s work; Provide regular updates to the Communications Director and other senior staff about the effectiveness of digital outreach and make recommendations for improvements; Manage a DC based press intern; Support the Communications Director and other staff on other duties as needed. Qualifications: 1-3 years experience in a communications role at a PR agency, political campaign, nonprofit, or similar relevant experience; Southern California ties and/or previous Hill experience preferred; Bachelor’s Degree; Strong knowledge of social media platforms, website management software, and digital marketing tools; Great eye for digital design and a finger on the pulse of the ever changing media landscape; Excellent writing and communication skills, with the ability to craft compelling content for diverse audiences; Strong attention to detail, ensuring accuracy and consistency in all digital content and communications; Ability to work independently, take initiative, and manage multiple tasks and long hours in a fast-paced environment. Application Process Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis, so applying early is strongly encouraged. How to Apply: Please send a resume, cover letter, three references and three digital samples to cisneros.ca31.jobs@gmail.com with the subject line: Full Name — Digital/Press Manager Application. Interested applicants are encouraged to apply as soon as possible. Our office is an equal opportunity employer, committed to non-discrimination based on race, color, religion, sex, disability, age, or national origin.

title: Communications Intern
description_text: The Office of Congresswoman Sarah McBride (D-DE) seeks a paid and highly motivated Communications Intern for Summer 2026 (June-August) based in the Washington, D.C. office. The Communications Intern works closely with a collaborative and creative Communications team to support a robust communications and digital strategy. The Communications Intern will also support other general office operations as assigned. Primary educational opportunities and responsibilities will include: Creating timely, engaging, and creative social media content highlighting Member’s legislative work, constituent advocacy wins, and community events; Producing high-quality graphics and videos via editing software; Drafting press releases, quotes, and other written materials; Maintaining internal clips lists; Compiling daily news clips; Media monitoring and researching; and Supporting the communications, legislative, and scheduling teams with other duties as assigned, including: Answering phones; Researching legislation for staff; and Attend briefings and hearings. The ideal candidate must be able to work in a fast-paced environment, have keen attention to detail, deadlines, and social media best practices and trends as well as previous experience in communications and digital creation. Candidates should highlight any experience in the following platforms in their application: Adobe Creative Suite, including Premiere Pro, Lightroom, and Photoshop Canva TVEyes Snapstream The office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, national origin, age, disability, military status, marital or parental status. Women, people of color, and other members of traditionally underrepresented communities are strongly encouraged to apply. Please send a cover letter, resume, writing sample (2 pages maximum) and 2-3 graphic or video samples in a single PDF to press.mcbride@mail.house.gov with the subject line “Communications Intern Summer 2026 - Last Name.” Applications close March 11, 2026.

title: Field Representative/Grants Coordinator
description_text: The Office of Congresswoman Luz Rivas (CA-29) is seeking a hardworking, organized, and reliable Field Representative/Grants Coordinator based in the San Fernando Valley in a full-time capacity. This is not an entry level position. This position is responsible for building and maintaining positive relationships with constituents, key community leaders and other stakeholders in the southern portion of the Congressional District. This position will monitor and update the District Director and relevant staff on important local issues, emerging trends and critical developments. Additional field responsibilities include attending events as the Member’s representative and staffing the Member, as determined by the District Director. Additionally, this role will oversee the Federal Grants Program which will develop, distribute and maintain monthly grant updates for stakeholders, and promote key successes within the community. This also includes planning and implementing a strong constituency-based outreach program by planning and executing successful grant workshops, drafting letters of support and outreach materials, and monitoring/notifying grant awards in the community. Occasionally this role may be assigned other duties. Ideal candidate must possess time management skills and the ability to multitask with attention to detail in a fast-paced environment. Candidates should have excellent written and oral communication skills, ability to work under pressure and a strong commitment to public service. Ability to think strategically and creatively to execute a proactive and informative outreach program. This role regularly interacts with constituents and stakeholders, and candidate should be Constituent Services Driven as well as have a friendly and professional demeanor.  Must possess the ability to exercise discretion and independent judgement. This position works closely with the District Director and alongside the District team, as well as the DC office to ensure strong communication and collaboration. The position requires the flexibility to work long hours. Diverse and bilingual candidates are encouraged to apply. Previous government/public sector and customer service experience are a plus. Knowledge of the southern part of the 29th Congressional District such as Toluca Lake and Valley Village is a plus. Annual salary is $55,000 - $65,000 and will depend on experience. There is a standard package of federal benefits, plus a Student Loan Repayment Program provided. To apply: Interested candidates are encouraged to submit a cover letter, resume, and writing sample in one PDF to ca29jobs@gmail.com with the following subject line: “Field Representative /Grants Coordinator - [candidate’s full name]”. This office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, sex (including marital or parental status), color, age, religion, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, or disability.

title: Legislative Director
description_text: Congresswoman Veronica Escobar, a member of the House Appropriations Committee, seeks a Legislative Director to lead the office’s legislative operation and oversee the development and execution of her policy agenda. The Legislative Director will play an essential leadership role in the office by working closely with the Chief of Staff in assisting with the formulation of the Member’s policy and legislative strategy. The Legislative Director will also work with the Communications Director to ensure that the Member’s legislative strategy is incorporated into the office’s communications plan. This position will also serve as the primary liaison to the House Appropriations Committee, coordinating the full appropriations process, community outreach to local stakeholders, along with managing the legislative team’s work. This position will also work closely with the District Director and Chief of Staff to ensure community project funding priorities are included and reflected throughout the appropriations process. This position will also maintain a portfolio of issues. This position requires long hours, weekends, frequent travel to the district, along with strong leadership and deep knowledge of the legislative process, with a emphasis on the appropriations process. This is not a entry level position (minimum 5 years as a legislative staffer). Texas ties and Spanish language proficiency are a plus. Women and minority candidates are encouraged to apply. Strong familiarity with the appropriations process is required. The Office of Congresswoman Veronica Escobar is an Equal Opportunity Employer who does not discriminate in employment on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy and gender identity), national origin, political affiliation, sexual orientation, marital status, disability, genetic information, age, membership in an employee organization, retaliation, parental status, military service, or other non-merit factor. Diverse candidates are encouraged to apply. Salary will be based on experience. Those interested in the position should email a resume, cover letter, and writing sample to tx16.jobs@mail.house.gov with the subject line “Legislative Director”. We will interview on a rolling basis.

title: Digital Communications Assistant
description_text: DIGITAL COMMUNICATIONS ASSISTANT – The office of Congressman Josh Riley is seeking a creative and detail-oriented Digital Communications Assistant based out of his Washington, D.C. office. The ideal applicant will have a passion for digital engagement across online and social media platforms and will bring intermediate level graphic design and video editing skills. Attention to detail is a must. Ties to Upstate New York are a plus, but not required. Responsibilities: Creating videos, graphics, and other digital content that is compelling, engaging, on-brand, and error-free Proactively drafting daily content in the voice of the Congressman and rapid response online messaging for breaking news Assisting with drafting and circulating the Congressman’s newsletter, statements, talking points, and speeches, online Taking high quality photos and video of the Congressman at events and in meetings Managing a strategic content calendar Compiling the Member’s daily news clips to distribute to office staff The salary range for this position is $55,000-$65,000, commensurate with experience. This position will sometimes require evening and weekend work and travel to the district in New York. The Digital Communication Assistant will report to the Communications Director based in Washington, D.C. To apply, please email rileyny19jobs@gmail.com with “Digital Communications Assistant” in the subject line and the following application materials attached a single combined PDF: Resume Two writing samples Portfolio of graphic and video design work Three social media content samples The office is an equal opportunity employer; we do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, national origin, disability, military status, age, marital status, or parental status. Candidates of diverse backgrounds are encouraged to apply.

title: Legislative Assistant
description_text: Congressman David Scott (GA-13), senior member of the House Financial Services Committee and House Agriculture Committee, seeks a Legislative Assistant to join his Washington, D.C. office to handle a portfolio focused on issues such as education, transportation & infrastructure, telecommunications, Homeland Security, immigration, FEMA, and other issues as assigned. Core responsibilities include: Researching and advancing legislative and policy priorities Drafting vote recommendations, memos, and talking points Monitoring legislation and Floor activity Staffing the Congressman for stakeholder meetings Representing the Congressman in meetings with constituents, advocates, and stakeholder groups. The Legislative Assistant will work closely with the Legislative Director and policy team to ensure alignment with the Congressman’s priorities and district needs. Georgia ties are preferred. Ideal candidates will have excellent writing and organizational skills. Interested candidates should e-mail a cover letter, resumé, and one writing sample to Georgia13Resumes@gmail.com with the subject line, “Last Name, First Name, Legislative Assistant”. The deadline for applying is Thursday, February 12th, COB. Salary range is commensurate with experience and includes health insurance, student loan repayment, and other benefits. The Office of Congressman David Scott is an Equal Opportunity Employer and does not discriminate in employment on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy and gender identity), national origin, political affiliation, sexual orientation, marital status, disability, genetic information, age, membership in an employee organization, retaliation, parental status, or military service. Diverse candidates are encouraged to apply.

title: Staff Assistant
description_text: Congresswoman Luz Rivas is seeking a highly-motivated, collaborative, and organized Staff Assistant based in Washington, D.C. in a full-time capacity. This is an entry-level position. This position is responsible for managing the front office, including answering phones and greeting visitors, as well as managing constituent tours and flag requests, coordinating the DC internship program, and assisting with administrative duties for the office, as needed. Additionally, this position will assist staff, as needed. Ideal candidates will have excellent writing, verbal, and organizational skills, as well as the ability to work well with others in a fast-paced environment. This role regularly interacts with constituents and visitors and should have a friendly and professional demeanor. Ties to California’s 29th Congressional District are preferred. Diverse and bilingual candidates are encouraged to apply. To apply: Interested candidates are encouraged to submit a cover letter, resume, and writing sample in one PDF document to CA29jobs@gmail.com with the following subject line: “DC Staff Assistant - [candidate’s full name]”. This office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, sex (including marital or parental status), color, age, religion, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, or disability.

title: Summer 2026 Internship
description_text: The Office of Congresswoman Marcy Kaptur (OH-09) is seeking motivated college students, recent graduates, and graduate students for the Summer 2026 Internship Program in her Washington, D.C., office. Intern responsibilities vary daily but typically include assisting with constituent services, sorting mail, answering phones, conducting legislative research, attending hearings and briefings, and providing general administrative support to staff. The ideal candidate will have strong written and oral communication skills, an interest in public service and the legislative process, and the ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment. Applicants should be organized, dependable, and eager to learn. Ties to Ohio are strongly preferred but not required. To Apply: Interested candidates should submit a single PDF containing the following materials: A current résumé A one-page cover letter outlining your interest in the position A short written response (300–500 words) to the following prompt: What does public service mean to you? You may share a personal experience, describe values you live by, or reflect on a moment that has shaped your interest in public service. Please send completed applications in a SINGLE PDF to angela.maloney@mail.house.gov with the subject line: Spring 2026 Internship Application – [Your Name] Application Deadline: March 2, 2026, at 12:00 pm EST This internship offers a unique opportunity to gain firsthand experience in a congressional office, develop professional skills, and contribute to the work of Congresswoman Kaptur and her team. The Office of Congresswoman Kaptur is an equal-opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, disability, military status, age, marital status, or parental status.

title: Legislative Assistant
description_text: Congresswoman Veronica Escobar (TX-16), member of the House Appropriations Committee, is seeking a Legislative Assistant position to join her Washington, D.C. team to handle a portfolio focused on domestic policy issues such as healthcare, energy and environment, tribal issues, agriculture, animals, and other issues as assigned. This is not an entry-level position; prior legislative and/or Capitol Hill experience is required. Core responsibilities include researching and advancing legislative and policy priorities; drafting vote recommendations, memos, and talking points; monitoring legislation and floor activity; preparing the Congresswoman for briefings and external meetings; and representing the office in meetings with constituents, advocates, and stakeholder groups. The Legislative Assistant will work closely with the Legislative Director and broader policy team to ensure alignment with the Congresswoman’s priorities and district needs. Texas ties and Spanish language proficiency are a plus. Women and minority candidates are encouraged to apply. Strong familiarity with the appropriations process is required. The Office of Congresswoman Veronica Escobar is an Equal Opportunity Employer who does not discriminate in employment on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy and gender identity), national origin, political affiliation, sexual orientation, marital status, disability, genetic information, age, membership in an employee organization, retaliation, parental status, military service, or other non-merit factor. Diverse candidates are encouraged to apply. Salary will be based on experience. Those interested in the position should email a resume, cover letter, and writing sample to tx16.jobs@mail.house.gov with the subject line “Legislative Assistant”. Deadline to apply is February 13th, 2026.

title: Press Secretary
description_text: Title: Press Secretary Position Type: Full-time Location: Garden City, NY Salary: $65,000 - $75,000 annually Summary: The Office of Congresswoman Laura Gillen is seeking a Press Secretary to be based in the district on Long Island. The ideal candidate has excellent writing, editing, and photography skills as well as experience building press relationships. They will assist the Communications Director in drafting releases, talking points, event remarks, and quotes, staffing the Member at district-based press events, and help craft digital content. Strong proofreading and copyediting skills are a must. The role requires the ability to operate efficiently under tight deadlines, occasionally work outside of traditional business hours, juggle multiple priorities, and work closely with colleagues across the D.C. and district offices. The Press Secretary will assist in maintaining relationships with in- state press and will report to the Communications Director. This is not an entry-level position. The ideal candidate will have 2-3 years of experience working with various digital platforms, working with reporters, writing, and managing social media accounts for a political candidate or elected official. Candidates should be detail-oriented and highly motivated to serve in a fast-paced environment. Ties to the district and Spanish fluency are a plus. Key responsibilities include: ·  Collaborate closely with the Congresswoman, Communications Director, and staff to craft cohesive and engaging messaging in local press and across digital platforms; ·  Draft and edit press releases, media advisories, statements, remarks, and daily press clips; ·  Support the Communications Director in responding to local and national media inquiries; ·  Help identify and cultivate relationships with local reporters and content creators in the district; ·  Staff and manage reporter interactions with the Member at district-based press events; ·  Manage recording and photography at district-based press events; ·  Work with the District Office and DC-based Press Assistant to craft content and publicize constituent services; ·  Edit and maintain the Member’s website to include relevant updates, press releases, news, photos, and more; ·  Lead the creation of the weekly newsletter to highlight the Congresswoman’s official work. The salary range for this position is $65,000-$75,000, depending on experience, with full benefits including up to $833/month in student loan repayments; health, dental, and vision insurance; and both defined-benefit and defined-contribution retirement plans. TO APPLY : Please email a brief cover letter, resume, and two writing samples in a single PDF to NY04Press@mail.house.gov with “Press Secretary –  [Your Name]” in the subject line. Applicants may also provide one example of their digital design work and/or photography (this is optional). Applications will be accepted on a rolling basis. The U.S. House of Representatives is an equal opportunity employer.

title: Digital Manager/Press Secretary
description_text: The Office of Congressman Ted W. Lieu (CA-36) seeks a creative and experienced Digital Manager/Press Secretary based in the Washington DC office. This role will support Congressman Lieu’s efforts as the Representative for California’s 36th Congressional District and Vice Chair of the House Democratic Caucus. This is not an entry-level position. The ideal candidate has worked in communications for two or more years. Experience in graphic design, photography/videography, and social media management is desired. Some of the daily tasks that the Digital Manager/Press Secretary will be responsible for are overseeing the office’s social media, creating digital content, photographing events, and drafting and sending press releases. The Office is looking for a candidate who possesses sound judgement, curiosity about new and emerging media trends, a good sense of humor, and a keen attention to detail. The Office is fast-paced and collaborative, and someone who can be proactive while balancing multiple projects will thrive. This position requires some non-traditional work hours and reports to the Deputy Chief of Staff. Salary range is $60,000-$70,000 commensurate with experience. Competitive federal benefits package. Candidates should submit the following to lieu.staff@mail.house.gov with the subject line “Digital Manager/Press Secretary”: Cover letter Resume Three references (name, title, phone number, email and relationship) One professional writing sample Graphics, videos, or photos you’ve produced in a professional capacity Social media copy you’ve drafted in a professional capacity No calls or drop-ins please. The Office of Congressman Lieu is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate against candidates on the basis of race, ethnicity, religion, sex, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability status, or veteran status.

title: Press Assistant/Legislative Correspondent
description_text: The Office of Congressman Lou Correa (CA-46) seeks a friendly, organized, and detail-oriented Press Assistant/Legislative Correspondent to serve as an integral part of our D.C. office, spearheading and supporting the Congressman’s outreach efforts in collaboration with both legislative and communications staff. This position is based out of the Congressman’s Washington, D.C. office and will report to the Communications Director. WHAT YOU WILL DO: Assist in intern/fellow management alongside the Communications Director. Oversee administrative tasks in the office and monitor tour operations. Sort and track incoming mail relying on the assistance of interns/fellows, while overseeing the drafting clear and concise letters and ensuring timely and accurate responses using our constituent correspondence system, Indigov. Work alongside the Communications Director to assist in the office’s trilingual proactive outreach efforts by writing engaging social copy, making creative digital content (including graphics, videos, newsletters, and 499s, etc.), compiling daily press clips, assisting in maintaining the Congressman’s website, and supporting the communications team as needed. WHAT YOU BRING: Strong commitment to the Congressman’s mission and values Excellent organization Exceptional attention to detail Great writing skills Discretion with sensitive information Previous Hill experience California ties (preferred) Spanish language proficiency (preferred) Vietnamese language proficiency (a plus!) APPLICATION PROCESS: You should send your resume, cover letter, and two writing samples in one PDF to CA46Jobs@mail.house.gov , with the subject line “First Name, Last Name – LC/PA” Applications will be considered on a rolling basis. Should you be selected for an interview, you will be contacted by a member of the Congressman’s D.C. office. Salary is competitive and commensurate with experience. ABOUT LOU: Congressman Lou Correa is a longtime Orange County resident, with deep local roots. To this day, he lives only three miles from his childhood neighborhood in Anaheim. He is the son of working-class parents whose hard work gave him a chance at success, and has spent his career fighting to protect the American Dream, and ensure anyone can reach the middle class, just as he did. In 2016, Lou was elected to the U.S. House of Representatives to continue his work by representing the community he has spent the past 20 years serving, fighting to give everyone access to the same opportunity he had. Congressman Correa is committed to working across party lines to strengthen the middle class and give everyone a shot at the American Dream by investing in education, healthcare, and our fading infrastructure, and has introduced legislation to protect the legal rights of immigrants, care for veterans, and fight against the wasteful spending of taxpayer money.

title: Communications Director
description_text: The Office of Congressman Lou Correa (CA-46) seeks a self-starting, experienced, and creative Communications Director to join his Washington, D.C. office and lead a fast-paced communications team. The ideal candidate will have familiarity with beltway + California press and the House franking process, and will work closely with the District Director, Legislative Director, and Chief of Staff to ensure successful execution of the Congressman’s messaging priorities and outreach efforts at home in Orange County and in Washington. This position is based out of the Congressman’s Washington, D.C. office and will report to the Chief of Staff. This position will also be the direct supervisor of a Press Assistant/Press Secretary. This is not an entry-level position. WHAT YOU WILL DO: Serve as the Congressman’s chief spokesperson and media liaison; Develop and implement strategic communications plans for the office, including working closely with the legislative team to develop media strategies to roll out legislation, send letters, and make other major announcements; Successfully and consistently pitch the Congressman to local and national TV, radio, and print media on issues consistent with his priorities; Prepare for, brief, and staff the Congressman for media interviews, press conferences, roundtables, and other engagements; Draft talking points, speeches, press releases, op-eds and other materials, as necessary, and in the Congressman’s voice; Oversee management of the Congressman’s website, newsletter, robust paid and franked tri-lingual constituent outreach efforts; And more. WHAT YOU BRING: 2-3+ years of communications experience Strong commitment to the Congressman’s mission and values Excellent organization Exceptional attention to detail Great writing skills Discretion with sensitive information California ties (preferred) Spanish language proficiency (preferred) Vietnamese language proficiency (a plus!) APPLICATION PROCESS: You should send your resume, cover letter, and two writing samples in one PDF to CA46Jobs@mail.house.gov , with the subject line “First Name, Last Name – Communications Director” Applications will be considered on a rolling basis. Should you be selected for an interview, you will be contacted by a member of the Congressman’s D.C. office. Salary is competitive and commensurate with experience. ABOUT LOU: Congressman Lou Correa is a longtime Orange County resident, with deep local roots. To this day, he lives only three miles from his childhood neighborhood in Anaheim. He is the son of working-class parents whose hard work gave him a chance at success, and has spent his career fighting to protect the American Dream, and ensure anyone can reach the middle class, just as he did. In 2016, Lou was elected to the U.S. House of Representatives to continue his work by representing the community he has spent the past 20 years serving, fighting to give everyone access to the same opportunity he had. Congressman Correa is committed to working across party lines to strengthen the middle class and give everyone a shot at the American Dream by investing in education, healthcare, and our fading infrastructure, and has introduced legislation to protect the legal rights of immigrants, care for veterans, and fight against the wasteful spending of taxpayer money.

title: Communications Director
description_text: Congresswoman Debbie Dingell (MI-06) seeks an experienced, creative, and highly motivated Communications Director to lead the office's fast-paced communications operations. The Communications Director will lead the development and execution of a comprehensive strategy encompassing national and local press, digital and new media, and franking. Responsibilities include but are not limited to: developing and maintaining relationships with reporters and producers, drafting press releases, talking points, speeches, and op-eds, identifying opportunities for proactive media outreach, responding to press inquiries, serving as on-the-record spokesperson, coordinating, preparing for, and staffing TV interviews and public speaking events, developing and implementing franked media plan, overseeing all digital operations, managing a DC-based Digital Director and district-based Digital Assistant. The Communications Director will work collaboratively with legislative and district staff and closely with the Member. This is a senior-level position. The ideal candidate will possess strong written and oral communications skills and the ability to work effectively in a fast-paced setting. They will have at least 2-4 years of experience as a press secretary, communications director, or similar communications role. Salary is commensurate with experience. All interested candidates are encouraged to submit a resume, cover letter, two writing samples, and references to dingell.jobs@mail.house.gov with the subject line "Communications Director - [Name]" The office will contact the best qualified candidates to begin our interview process on a rolling basis. Please, no phone calls or drop ins. The office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, national origin, age, disability, military status, marital or parental status. Women, people of color, and other members of traditionally underrepresented communities are strongly encouraged to apply.

title: Communications Director
description_text: Congressmember Judy Chu (CA-28) seeks an experienced Communications Director based in her Washington office to lead a robust press and communications operation. The Communications Director will serve as the Member’s primary speechwriter and will lead the development and execution of a comprehensive press and communications strategy. This position is part of the office’s senior staff and will be responsible for managing a Press Secretary and Staff/Press Assistant. Responsibilities include but are not limited to: Drafting and editing speeches, talking points, press releases, and op-eds Coordinating with press and preparing and staffing the Member for interviews Developing and carrying out a mass communications strategy Overseeing digital communications including social media, graphics, and videos Establishing and maintaining relationships with national and local reporters and producers Working closely with both district and DC staff This position is intended for experienced communications staff who work quickly and accurately in a fast-paced setting, and will contribute to a positive and supportive work environment as part of a close-knit team. Strong writing and editing skills are a must. Candidates with experience working in a congressional office are strongly encouraged to apply. Salary will be competitive and commensurate with experience. The office offers federal student loan repayment, paid vacation, and paid sick leave, in addition to retirement and health care benefits. This is not an entry level position. Applicants should be experienced communications professionals. To apply, please email a cover letter, resume, and a writing sample as one combined PDF with the subject line “[LAST NAME] – Communications Director” to CA28Jobs@mail.house.gov . The office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, national origin, age, disability, military status, marital or parental status.

title: Press Internship
description_text: The Office of Congressman Hakeem Jeffries (NY-08) seeks highly motivated Press interns to work in-person in a fast-paced leadership office. Responsibilities include compiling daily press clips, drafting preparation materials for media hits and assisting with press-related events. Applicants may occasionally be asked to work additional morning or evening hours. The term of this paid internship will be from May to Late July/August 2026. Applicants must be able to work in Washington, D.C. Ideal applicants must have a professional and positive attitude, strong research and writing skills and a detail-oriented work ethic. To apply, please upload your application materials HERE . Women, people of color, LGBTQIA+ individuals, veterans and those with disabilities are encouraged to apply. The deadline to submit application is Friday, February 27, 2026 at 5:00 p.m. ET. Absolutely no phone calls or drop-ins please. All interns must be high school graduates who are enrolled in or have recently graduated from an accredited post-secondary educational institution, college or university.

title: Executive and Personal Office Internship
description_text: The Office of Congressman Hakeem Jeffries (NY-08) is seeking motivated, detail-oriented interns to work full-time, in-person in a fast-paced leadership and personal office. Intern responsibilities include administrative work, research, event planning and assisting the Executive and Personal Office. Applicants must be able to work 40 hours per week in Washington, D.C. and may occasionally be asked to work additional morning or evening hours. The term of this paid internship will be from May to Late July/August 2026. Applicants must be able to work in Washington, D.C. Ideal applicants must have a professional and positive attitude, strong research and writing skills and a detail-oriented work ethic. To apply, please upload your application materials HERE . Women, people of color, LGBTQIA+ individuals, veterans and those with disabilities are encouraged to apply. The deadline to submit application is Friday, February 27, 2026 at 5:00 p.m. ET. Absolutely no phone calls or drop-ins please. All interns must be high school graduates who are enrolled in or have recently graduated from an accredited post-secondary educational institution, college or university.

title: Legislative Director
description_text: Congressman Josh Harder, a Member of the Appropriations Committee and Chair of the Build America Caucus, seeks a Legislative Director to oversee his legislative team and drive a fast-paced policy agenda. As the leader of the legislative team, the Legislative Director will be driven by three core pillars: championing the Build America abundance agenda, earning tangible wins for our district and managing and mentoring staff. We are proud to be one of the most diverse congressional teams in the country. Experience and Qualifications You have a track record of actively managing, mentoring, and developing staff. You thrive on building collaborative, team-first environments. You are excited about strategically building out the Build America Caucus abundance policy portfolio (housing, energy, infrastructure). You have a history of moving legislation across the finish line and securing wins. You are a strategic thinker with a thorough knowledge of the legislative process who can see the big picture while managing daily operations. You work quickly under pressure and manage time efficiently for yourself and the team. You have 6+ years of policy experience including on the Hill. Responsibilities Manage, mentor, and guide a legislative team of four, serving as a resource for their professional growth and legislative strategy. Develop and oversee the Member’s entire legislative agenda, providing recommendations on all policy positions, legislation, and votes. Drive the policy strategy and stakeholder engagement for the Build America Caucus and Appropriations Committee. Work closely with the district team and local stakeholders to deliver appropriations wins and tangible results for the community. Collaborate with the communications team to successfully message bills. Handle a tailored portfolio of legislative issues based on expertise. To Apply: Email a cover letter and resume in a single PDF to harder.resumes@mail.house.gov with the subject line, “Legislative Director” by Friday, January 30th, 2026. Our office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, national origin, disability, military status, age, marital status, or parental status. Candidates from diverse backgrounds are strongly encouraged to apply.

title: Digital Director
description_text: The Office of Congressman Chris Deluzio seeks a highly motivated, experienced, and creative Digital Director to join an active communications team. This position is based in the Washington, D.C. The Digital Director is responsible for developing a strong online presence, capturing the Congressman’s work and activity, and amplifying that through local media, digital content, and constituent communications. The ideal candidate will be creative, have strong proficiency in crafting digital content, fluency on all channels of social media, video editing experience, be a strong writer, and be a detail-oriented team player able to handle multiple tasks at once. In this position, the ability to hit deadlines along with excellent writing, organizational, and social media skills are essential. Ideal candidates are self-starters who can successfully work in a fast-paced environment while collaborating in a small team. Ties to Western PA, flexibility, and a sense of humor are greatly appreciated. The position is based in D.C. and reports to the Communications Director. Key Qualifications: Lead social media, email, graphic, branding, and multimedia strategy. Draft, pitch, and execute social media content working with the Communications Director and Member. Draft and distribute press releases, statements, and advisories. Coordinate digital campaigns tied to legislative and district initiatives. Manage content calendar and analytics across all platforms. Assist in preparing the Congressman for interviews and media events. Manage the technology and software needed for interviews, podcasts, video filming, and other recordings. Collaborate with the Communications Director on message framing and rapid response. Develop and manage digital content across platforms (Facebook, Instagram, X, Bluesky, Threads, and YouTube). Support the office’s social media strategy by drafting social media posts and producing video and graphic materials. Update and manage the official website. Monitor national and local media coverage, as well as social media trends. Clip, edit, and caption TV appearances, floor speeches, long & short form videos, and more. Ties to Western Pennsylvania or the Rust Belt. Experience with video editing, on social media, Adobe, Kapwing, Canva, or other platforms. Strong writer and editor who stays abreast of breaking news and social media trends. Graphic design expertise with Canva, Adobe Suite, and/or similar platforms. Understanding of social media trends, ability to analyze data, and work with digital vendors to decide how to deliver the Congressman’s message most effectively. Availability to work long hours as needed, including outside of the 9-5 workday. Ability to work well under pressure and to work cooperatively/courteously with others. Regular tasks include: Planning, strategizing, managing, and creating content for the member’s social media platforms and website. Graphic design, photography, video filming, and video editing. Organizing and storing photos, videos, clips, and press lists. Drafting releases, talking points, franked communications, newsletters, and advisories. organizing and implementing press events. Capturing content at events. Research and pitch collaborations with digital content creators and podcast hosts. Any member of Congressman Deluzio’s team should possess the following characteristics: Willingness to work flexible hours. This will not always be a 9 to 5 job. Commitment to servant leadership. Represent and “live” the Western PA values of our team. Growth mindset, openness to feedback, and desire to grow with the team. Hustle, grit, and perseverance. Ability to think critically and make sound decisions. Ability to problem solve and execute solutions. Excellent oral and written communications skills and organizational abilities. Heightened relationship management and project management experience. This is not an entry-level position and is based in Washington D.C. Qualified candidates should submit a resume (.pdf), writing sample (.pdf), an example of a previous graphic design project, and an example of a previous video project by email to deluziojobs@gmail.com with “Digital Director” in the subject line. No phone calls or drop-ins please. This is office is an equal opportunity employer; we do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, national origin or citizenship status, sex, gender identity or expression, pregnancy sexual orientation, age, disability, or military status.

title: Press Assistant/Staff Assistant
description_text: The Office of Congresswoman Suzan DelBene (WA-01) seeks a dynamic digital press assistant/staff assistant with strong new media skills. Responsibilities include sending daily clips, developing social/digital media content, drafting press materials, and assisting in other aspects of the team’s fast-paced media operation. Strong writing skills and a proven track record with digital media are required. Staff assistant duties include maintaining the front office, overseeing tours and flag requests, and ensuring day-to-day operations run smoothly. Hill experience and Washington state ties are a plus. If you have salary requirements, please include them in your application. The office is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, gender identity or expression, or sexual orientation. Diverse candidates are strongly encouraged to apply. Please mention any Pacific Northwest ties in your cover letter. The salary for this position starts at $65,000. To apply, please send your cover letter, resume, and two 1-2 page writing samples or a digital portfolio to Washington.Resumes@mail.house.gov and put “Digital Press Assistant – [NAME]” in the subject line.

title: Communications Fellow
description_text: THE OFFICE: The Office of Congresswoman Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick, a member of the House Foreign Affairs Committee (HFAC) and the House Veteran Affairs Committee (HVAC), seeks a strategic, detail-oriented, hardworking, and highly organized Communications Fellow to join our Communications team. The office represents Florida’s 20th Congressional District and is committed to delivering results for constituents through economic stability, healthcare access, housing affordability, and opportunity for all. Our office values professionalism, execution, and people-centered communication. THE FELLOWSHIP: This fellowship is ideal for a candidate who has worked in communications, advocacy, media, or related fields and want to deepen their understanding of federal policy, legislative processes, and public service while contributing meaningfully to the day-to-day work of a Member of Congress. The Communications Fellow will support the Communications Department in executing the Congresswoman’s messaging, digital presence, and media operations. This role offers hands-on experience in congressional communications, strategic messaging, and public engagement at a critical moment for the district. THE CANDIDATE: The ideal candidate is a professional with communications experience seeking to understand Capitol Hill; a strong writer, detail-oriented, highly organized, has stellar writing and verbal communication skills, exceptional organizational skills, and eager to learn how a congressional office communicates clearly, responsibly, and effectively. The ideal candidate will have proven knowledge of the communications industry, and ability to balance competing demands. A minimum of 2-4 years of communications experience strongly preferred. South Florida ties is a plus. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: • Draft and edit official communications including press releases, statements, advisories, newsletters, constituent updates, social media copy and digital content; • Assist with media monitoring, daily press clips, and rapid response tracking; • Support content development for district and Washington communications; • Help maintain message discipline and brand consistency across platforms; • Assist with preparation for press events, briefings, and public appearances; • Coordinate with legislative, district, and scheduling teams as needed. QUALIFICIATIONS: • Strong writing and editing skills (required); • Interest in public service, communications, journalism, or public policy; • Ability to work in a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment; • Strong attention to detail and organizational skills; • Familiarity with social media platforms and digital communications; • Ability to exercise sound judgment and professionalism; • Prior internship or experience in communications, media, or government; • Familiarity with Adobe, Canva, or basic content management tools; • Ties to or understanding of Florida’s 20th Congressional District. PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT OPPORTUNITIES: • Exposure to congressional press strategy and operations; • Mentorship from senior communications staff; • Opportunities to publish written work and contribute to live projects; • Insight into media relations, messaging, and public narrative management. This position is based in Washington, DC. TO APPLY: Applications will be accepted until the position is filled. The office will contact the best qualified candidates to begin the interview process. To apply, please email FL20Jobs@mail.house.gov with “Communications Fellow” in the subject line and the following materials: (1) cover letter, (2) resume, (3) transcript, (4) two references including name, current title and organization, phone number and email.

title: Legislative Assistant
description_text: Rep. Morgan McGarvey (KY-03) has an immediate need to hire an experienced full-time Legislative Assistant in the Washington, D.C. office. The Legislative Assistant will oversee the Member’s work on healthcare, education, labor, pensions, and other issues for the office. They will work closely with the Legislative Director and Chief of Staff to develop and execute a coordinated legislative strategy for the Member. The Legislative Assistant will prepare the Member for Committee hearings and markups and attend meetings with constituents, visitors, and other VIPs. The position is full time with federal benefits, including student loan repayment program (if eligible), transit or parking benefits, and an annual salary between $65k-$70k commensurate with experience. Job Duties: Works with the Legislative Director and Chief of Staff to formulate and advance legislative initiatives for assigned issue areas Tracks legislation and coordinates co-sponsorship of bills for the Member Briefs the Member on bills before the House, provides advice on Committee activity, and assists the district office staff with legislative matters that impact the local community Provides input to help the Communications Director to draft speeches, press releases, op-eds, and Congressional Record statements for the Member Represents the Member at meetings with constituents and interest groups Provides district office staff with targeted information for community events and Town Hall meetings Other duties as assigned Requirements: Experience researching public policy and preparing recommendations for a Member of Congress or other executives/leaders (e.g., Federal department/agency, non-profit organization, private sector) Strong written and verbal communication skills Experience making decisions and advising Principals on important issues Comfortable navigating challenging situations and being politically savvy Prior experience with forming partnerships and advancing legislative priorities for a Member of Congress Prioritization and time management skills, with the ability to manage multiple projects at a time Knowledge of issues in portfolio Preferences: Ties to the district or Kentucky strongly preferred Bachelor’s degree in government, political science, public policy, or related field A demonstrated interest in public service To Apply: All interested candidates are encouraged to submit a resume, writing sample, and references by resumesky03@gmail.com with the subject line “Legislative Assistant - [applicant’s last name]” by Thursday, January 29 . Please indicate if you have ties to the district or Kentucky in your email.  Please no walk-ins or phone calls. Our office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including marital or parental status), national origin, age, disability, gender identity, sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. Our office encourages all qualified candidates, including those with diverse backgrounds, to apply. Applicants must be eligible for employment with the United States Government.

title: Digital/ Press Assistant
description_text: The Office of Congressman Glenn Ivey (MD-04) seeks a Digital/ Press Assistant for his Washington, DC office. Ideal candidates will have strong writing, editing and digital skills including video editing and graphic design. Must be able to quickly develop creative and punchy digital content. This position reports to the Communications Director and will also work closely with the office’s Chief of Staff. Responsibilities: Develop and implement the office’s digital and social media strategy Film and edit videos for the Member Assist with drafting press releases, talking points, scripts, op-eds/letters to the editor, newsletters, and other written materials Work closely with the Chief of Staff to implement constituent outreach. Manage daily clips for the office Develop and update website content Track social media performance and produce clear, monthly reports on engagement, reach, and growth. Qualifications: Proficient in graphic design and video editing Experience with various digital platforms, managing social media accounts, maintaining websites, and e-newsletter creation and distribution A strong knowledge of Adobe Suite, Canva, and other content creation tools Creativity, attention to detail, and strong writing skills are a must Entrepreneurial skills and ability to work in a fast-paced environment Ability to work collaboratively as a team player Some experience with franked communications preferred but not required Capitol Hill experience is preferred but not required Maryland ties are a plus The salary range for this position is $55,000 - $65,000, commensurate with experience. The office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, national origin, disability, military status, age, marital status, or parental status. We encourage candidates from diverse backgrounds to apply. Candidates should submit a resume, cover letter, 2 digital samples and 1 writing sample, and 3 references in a single PDF with the subject line “Digital/Press Assistant - [Your Name] ” to: MD04Resumes@gmail.com . Applications will be accepted until Friday, February 6th, at 5:00PM ET. No phone calls, faxes, or drop-ins, please.

title: Legislative Director
description_text: Background: Congresswoman Summer L. Lee (D-Pittsburgh) has an immediate need to hire an experienced Legislative Director in the Washington D.C. office at a full-time capacity. Interested candidates are encouraged to submit a resume and cover letter by 2/15 . The office will contact the best qualified candidates to begin our interview process on a rolling basis. Job Expectations: The Legislative Director plays an essential leadership role in the office by working closely with the Chief of Staff in assisting with the formulation of the Member’s policy and legislative strategy. The Legislative Director will maintain status reports on all legislation affecting the district and legislation in which the Member is a principal sponsor or cosponsor. The Legislative Director may also manage and oversee the performance of the legislative team (e.g., legislative assistant, legislative correspondent). Congresswoman Lee currently sits on the House Committee on Oversight and Accountability and the House Committee on Education and the Workforce.
